2: // Connects to OpenCode's SSE endpoint and emits typed events.
3: // Uses sse-backoff.ts for reconnection logic and event-translator.ts for parsing.

35: // ─── SSE Consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

37: export class SSEConsumer extends EventEmitter<SSEConsumerEvents> {
38: 	private readonly baseUrl: string;
39: 	private readonly authHeaders: Record<string, string>;
40: 	private readonly backoffConfig: BackoffConfig;
41: 	private readonly healthTracker: HealthTracker;
42: 
43: 	private abortController: AbortController | null = null;
44: 	private reconnectAttempt = 0;
45: 	private running = false;
46: 	private reconnectTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
47: 
48: 	constructor(options: SSEConsumerOptions) {
49: 		super();
50: 		this.baseUrl = options.baseUrl.replace(/\/+$/, "");
51: 		this.authHeaders = options.authHeaders ?? {};
52: 
53: 		this.backoffConfig = {
54: 			baseDelay: options.backoff?.baseDelay ?? 1000,
55: 			maxDelay: options.backoff?.maxDelay ?? 30000,
56: 			multiplier: options.backoff?.multiplier ?? 2,
57: 		};
58: 
59: 		this.healthTracker = createHealthTracker({
60: 			staleThreshold: options.staleThreshold ?? 60_000,
61: 		});
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	/** Start consuming SSE events. Does not throw — errors are emitted. */
65: 	async connect(): Promise<void> {
66: 		if (this.running) return;
67: 		this.running = true;
68: 		this.reconnectAttempt = 0;
69: 		// Fire-and-forget: startStream handles its own errors via emit + reconnect.
70: 		// Catch edge cases where startStream throws before its internal try/catch.
71: 		this.startStream().catch((err) => {
72: 			if (!this.running) return;
73: 			const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));
74: 			this.emit("error", error);
75: 			this.scheduleReconnect();
76: 		});
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	/** Stop consuming and clean up */
80: 	async disconnect(): Promise<void> {
81: 		this.running = false;
82: 		if (this.reconnectTimer) {
83: 			clearTimeout(this.reconnectTimer);
84: 			this.reconnectTimer = null;
85: 		}
86: 		if (this.abortController) {
87: 			this.abortController.abort();
88: 			this.abortController = null;
89: 		}
90: 		this.healthTracker.onDisconnected();
91: 	}

94: 	getHealth(): ConnectionHealth & { stale: boolean } {
95: 		return this.healthTracker.getHealth();
96: 	}

99: 	isConnected(): boolean {

105: 	private async startStream(): Promise<void> {
106: 		if (!this.running) return;
107: 
108: 		const url = `${this.baseUrl}/event`;
109: 		this.abortController = new AbortController();
110: 
111: 		try {
112: 			const response = await fetch(url, {
113: 				headers: {

118: 				signal: this.abortController.signal,
119: 			});
120: 
121: 			if (!response.ok) {
122: 				throw new SSEConnectionError(
123: 					`SSE connection failed: HTTP ${response.status}`,
124: 					{ context: { url, status: response.status } },

126: 			}

128: 			if (!response.body) {
129: 				throw new SSEConnectionError("SSE response has no body", {

132: 			}

145: 			if (error.name === "AbortError") return; // Intentional abort

147: 			this.healthTracker.onDisconnected();

149: 			this.emit("error", error);
150: 
151: 			// Schedule reconnection

153: 		}
154: 	}

163: 				const { done, value } = await reader.read();
164: 				if (done) break;
165: 
166: 				buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
167: 
168: 				// Process complete SSE messages (double newline separated)
169: 				const messages = buffer.split("\n\n");
170: 				buffer = messages.pop() ?? "";
171: 
172: 				for (const message of messages) {
173: 					this.processSSEMessage(message);
174: 				}
175: 			}
176: 		} finally {
177: 			reader.releaseLock();
178: 		}
179: 
180: 		// Stream ended — reconnect if still running
181: 		if (this.running) {
182: 			this.healthTracker.onDisconnected();
183: 			this.emit("disconnected", undefined);
184: 			this.scheduleReconnect();
185: 		}
186: 	}
187: 
188: 	private processSSEMessage(raw: string): void {
189: 		// Parse SSE format: "event: type\ndata: {...}\n"
190: 		const lines = raw.split("\n");
191: 		let _eventType = "";
192: 		const dataLines: string[] = [];
193: 
194: 		for (const line of lines) {
195: 			if (line.startsWith("event:")) {
196: 				_eventType = line.slice(6).trim();
197: 			} else if (line.startsWith("data:")) {
198: 				dataLines.push(line.slice(5).trim());
199: 			} else if (line.startsWith(":")) {
200: 			}
201: 		}
202: 
203: 		if (dataLines.length === 0) {
204: 			// Heartbeat or empty message
205: 			if (raw.trim().startsWith(":")) {
206: 				this.healthTracker.onEvent();
207: 				this.emit("heartbeat");
208: 			}
209: 			return;
210: 		}
211: 
212: 		const dataStr = dataLines.join("\n");
213: 		const parsed = parseSSEDataAuto(dataStr);
214: 
215: 		if (!parsed.ok || !parsed.event) {
216: 			// Malformed event — log warning but continue (AC5)
217: 			return;
218: 		}
219: 
220: 		const event = parsed.event;
221: 
222: 		// Handle heartbeat events
223: 		if (
224: 			event.type === "server.heartbeat" ||
225: 			event.type === "server.connected"
226: 		) {
227: 			this.healthTracker.onEvent();
228: 			if (event.type === "server.connected") {
229: 				this.emit("heartbeat");
230: 			}
231: 			return;
232: 		}
233: 
234: 		// Track event for health
235: 		this.healthTracker.onEvent();
236: 
237: 		// Emit the event
238: 		this.emit("event", event);
239: 	}
240: 
241: 	private scheduleReconnect(): void {
242: 		if (!this.running) return;
243: 
244: 		const delay = calculateBackoffDelay(
245: 			this.reconnectAttempt,
246: 			this.backoffConfig,
247: 		);
248: 		this.reconnectAttempt++;
249: 
250: 		this.emit("reconnecting", {
251: 			attempt: this.reconnectAttempt,
252: 			delay,
253: 		});
254: 
255: 		this.healthTracker.onReconnect();
256: 
257: 		this.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
258: 			this.reconnectTimer = null;
259: 			this.startStream();
260: 		}, delay);
261: 	}
262: }

<content>1: // ─── SSE Event Wiring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts: the pipeline that takes SSE events from
3: // OpenCode, translates them, filters by session, records to cache, broadcasts
4: // to browser clients, and sends push notifications.
5: 
6: import type { Translator } from "./event-translator.js";
7: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
8: import {
9: 	hasInfoWithSessionID,
10: 	hasPartWithSessionID,
11: 	hasSessionID,
12: 	isPermissionRepliedEvent,
13: 	isSessionErrorEvent,
14: } from "./opencode-events.js";
15: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
16: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
17: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
18: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
19: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
20: import type { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
21: import type { OpenCodeEvent, RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
22: 
23: // ─── Session ID extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: // OpenCode SSE events store sessionID in different locations by event type:
25: //   - Top-level: message.part.delta, session.status, message.part.removed, etc.
26: //   - Nested in part: message.part.updated → properties.part.sessionID
27: //   - Nested in info: message.updated → properties.info.sessionID
28: // We must check all locations to correctly attribute events to sessions.
29: 
30: export function extractSessionId(event: OpenCodeEvent): string | undefined {
31: 	const props = event.properties;
32: 	// 1. Top-level sessionID (most common)
33: 	if (hasSessionID(props)) {
34: 		return props.sessionID;
35: 	}
36: 	// 2. Nested in part (message.part.updated)
37: 	if (hasPartWithSessionID(props)) {
38: 		return props.part.sessionID;
39: 	}
40: 	// 3. Nested in info (message.updated, session.updated)
41: 	if (hasInfoWithSessionID(props)) {
42: 		return props.info.sessionID ?? props.info.id;
43: 	}
44: 	return undefined;
45: }
46: 
47: // ─── Cache-eligible event types ──────────────────────────────────────────────
48: // Only chat-relevant events should be recorded to the message cache.
49: // Non-chat events (file_changed, permission_*, todo_state, etc.) would create
50: // false-positive cache hits that skip the REST API fallback on session switch,
51: // causing pre-relay conversation history to be lost.
52: 
53: const CACHEABLE_TYPES = new Set([
54: 	"user_message",
55: 	"delta",
56: 	"thinking_start",
57: 	"thinking_delta",
58: 	"thinking_stop",
59: 	"tool_start",
60: 	"tool_executing",
61: 	"tool_result",
62: 	"result",
63: 	"status",
64: 	"done",
65: 	"error",
66: ]);
67: 
68: export function isCacheable(msg: { type: string }): boolean {
69: 	return CACHEABLE_TYPES.has(msg.type);
70: }
71: 
72: // ─── SSE Wiring Dependencies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
73: 
74: export interface SSEWiringDeps {
75: 	translator: Translator;
76: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
77: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
78: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
79: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
80: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
81: 	wsHandler: {
82: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
83: 	};
84: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
85: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
86: }
87: 
88: // ─── Handle a single SSE event ───────────────────────────────────────────────
89: 
90: export function handleSSEEvent(
91: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
92: 	event: OpenCodeEvent,
93: ): void {
94: 	const {
95: 		translator,
96: 		sessionMgr,
97: 		messageCache,
98: 		permissionBridge,
99: 		questionBridge,
100: 		overrides,
101: 		wsHandler,
102: 		pushManager,
103: 		log,
104: 	} = deps;
105: 
106: 	const eventSessionId = extractSessionId(event);
107: 	log(`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"}`);
108: 
109: 	// ── Permission / question bridge routing ──────────────────────────────
110: 
111: 	if (event.type === "permission.asked") {
112: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionRequest(event);
113: 		if (pushManager) {
114: 			const id =
115: 				typeof event.properties.id === "string"
116: 					? event.properties.id
117: 					: "unknown";
118: 			const tool =
119: 				typeof event.properties.tool === "string"
120: 					? event.properties.tool
121: 					: typeof event.properties.permission === "string"
122: 						? event.properties.permission
123: 						: "A tool";
124: 			pushManager
125: 				.sendToAll({
126: 					type: "permission_request",
127: 					title: "Permission Needed",
128: 					body: `${tool} needs approval`,
129: 					tag: `perm-${id}`,
130: 				})
131: 				.catch(() => {});
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	if (event.type === "question.asked") {
135: 		questionBridge.onQuestion(event);
136: 		if (pushManager) {
137: 			pushManager
138: 				.sendToAll({
139: 					type: "ask_user",
140: 					title: "Question from Claude",
141: 					body: "Claude has a question for you",
142: 					tag: "opencode-ask",
143: 				})
144: 				.catch(() => {});
145: 		}
146: 	}
147: 	if (isPermissionRepliedEvent(event)) {
148: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionReplied(event.properties.id);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// ── Log session errors for debugging ──────────────────────────────────
152: 
153: 	if (isSessionErrorEvent(event)) {
154: 		const err = event.properties.error;
155: 		log(
156: 			`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"} Session error: ${err?.name ?? "?"} — ${err?.data?.message ?? "(no message)"}`,
157: 		);
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	// ── Translate → filter → cache → broadcast ───────────────────────────
161: 
162: 	const messages = translator.translate(event);
163: 	if (!messages) return;
164: 
165: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
166: 	const isActiveSession = !eventSessionId || eventSessionId === activeId;
167: 
168: 	const toSend = Array.isArray(messages) ? messages : [messages];
169: 	for (const msg of toSend) {
170: 		// Clear processing timeout when done event arrives for active session
171: 		if (msg.type === "done" && isActiveSession) {
172: 			overrides.clearProcessingTimeout();
173: 		}
174: 
175: 		// Only record chat-relevant events to the cache
176: 		const recordId = eventSessionId ?? activeId;
177: 		if (recordId && isCacheable(msg)) {
178: 			messageCache.recordEvent(recordId, msg);
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		// Only broadcast events for the active session to browser clients
182: 		if (!isActiveSession) continue;
183: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
184: 
185: 		// Push notifications for done/error events
186: 		if (pushManager) {
187: 			if (msg.type === "done") {
188: 				pushManager
189: 					.sendToAll({
190: 						type: "done",
191: 						title: "Task Complete",
192: 						body: "Claude has finished processing.",
193: 						tag: "opencode-done",
194: 					})
195: 					.catch(() => {});
196: 			}
197: 			if (msg.type === "error") {
198: 				pushManager
199: 					.sendToAll({
200: 						type: "error",
201: 						title: "Error",
202: 						body: msg.message || "An error occurred",
203: 						tag: "opencode-error",
204: 					})
205: 					.catch(() => {});
206: 			}
207: 		}
208: 	}
209: }
210: 
211: // ─── Wire all SSE consumer event listeners ───────────────────────────────────
212: 
213: export function wireSSEConsumer(
214: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
215: 	consumer: SSEConsumer,
216: ): void {
217: 	const { log } = deps;
218: 
219: 	consumer.on("connected", () =>
220: 		log(
221: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Connected to OpenCode event stream`,
222: 		),
223: 	);
224: 	consumer.on("disconnected", (err) =>
225: 		log(
226: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Disconnected${err ? `: ${err.message}` : ""}`,
227: 		),
228: 	);
229: 	consumer.on("reconnecting", ({ attempt, delay }) =>
230: 		log(
231: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Reconnecting (attempt ${attempt}, ${delay}ms delay)…`,
232: 		),
233: 	);
234: 	consumer.on("error", (err) =>
235: 		log(
236: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Error: ${err.message}`,
237: 		),
238: 	);
239: 
240: 	consumer.on("event", (event: OpenCodeEvent) => {
241: 		handleSSEEvent(deps, event);
242: 	});
243: }

32: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
33: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
34: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
35: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
36: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
37: 
38: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
39: export interface ProjectRelay {
40: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
41: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
42: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
43: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
44: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
45: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
46: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
47: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
48: 	stop(): Promise<void>;

51: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: export interface RelayStackConfig {
54: 	port: number;
55: 	host?: string;
56: 	opencodeUrl: string;
57: 	pin?: string;
58: 	projectDir: string;
59: 	slug: string;
60: 	staticDir?: string;
61: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
62: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
63: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
64: 	sessionTitle?: string;
65: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
66: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
67: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
68: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;

71: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: 
73: export interface RelayStack {
74: 	server: RelayServer;
75: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
76: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;

83: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
84: 	getPort(): number;

86: 	getBaseUrl(): string;

88: 	stop(): Promise<void>;

91: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: /**
94:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
95:  *
96:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
97:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
98:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
99:  *
100:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
101:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
102:  */
103: export async function createProjectRelay(
104: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
105: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
106: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
107: 
108: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
111: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
112: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
113: 	});
114: 	const translator = createTranslator();
115: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
116: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
117: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
118: 
119: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
120: 	const cacheDir = join(
121: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
122: 		".opencode-relay",
123: 		"sessions",
124: 	);
125: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
126: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
127: 
128: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
129: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
130: 
131: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
132: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
133: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
134: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
135: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
136: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
137: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
138: 
139: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
140: 
141: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
142: 		ptyId: string,
143: 		cursor: number = 0,
144: 	): Promise<void> {
145: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
146: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
147: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
148: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
149: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
150: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
151: 			config.opencodeUrl,
152: 		);
153: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
154: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
155: 
156: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
157: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
158: 				headers,
159: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
160: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
161: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
162: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
163: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
164: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
165: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
166: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
167: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
168: 			});
169: 
170: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
171: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
172: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
173: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
174: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
175: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
176: 			let connected = false;
177: 
178: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
179: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
180: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
181: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
182: 			}, 10_000);
183: 
184: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
185: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
186: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
187: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
188: 				let buf: Buffer;
189: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
190: 					buf = data;
191: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
192: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
193: 				} else {
194: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
195: 				}
196: 
197: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
198: 				const text = buf.toString();
199: 				if (!text) return;
200: 
201: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
202: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
203: 
204: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
205: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
206: 			});
207: 
208: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
209: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
210: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
211: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
212: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
213: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
214: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
215: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
216: 						type: "pty_exited",
217: 						ptyId,
218: 						exitCode: 0,
219: 					});
220: 				}
221: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
222: 			});
223: 
224: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
225: 				if (!connected) {
226: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
227: 					// Remove the failed session
228: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
229: 					reject(err);
230: 				}
231: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
232: 			});
233: 
234: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
235: 				connected = true;
236: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
237: 				resolve();
238: 			});
239: 		});
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243: 
244: 	await client.getHealth();
245: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
246: 
247: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248: 
249: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
250: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
251: 	);
252: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
253: 
254: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
255: 
256: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
257: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
258: 		{
259: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
260: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
261: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
262: 		},
263: 	);
264: 
265: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
266: 		wsHandler,
267: 		client,
268: 		sessionMgr,
269: 		messageCache,
270: 		overrides,
271: 		ptyManager,
272: 		log,
273: 	};
274: 
275: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
276: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
277: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
278: 			log(
279: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
280: 			),
281: 		);
282: 	});
283: 
284: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
285: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
286: 	});
287: 
288: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
289: 
290: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
291: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
292: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
293: 	});
294: 
295: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
296: 
297: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
298: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
299: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
300: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
301: 			if (switchId) {
302: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
303: 				const hasChatContent =
304: 					events?.some(
305: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
306: 					) ?? false;
307: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
308: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
309: 					return;
310: 				}
311: 			}
312: 		}
313: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
314: 	});
315: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
316: 		translator.reset();
317: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
318: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
319: 		try {
320: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
321: 			const parts: Array<{
322: 				parts?: Array<{
323: 					id: string;
324: 					type: PartType;
325: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
326: 				}>;
327: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
328: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
329: 					| Array<{
330: 							id: string;
331: 							type: PartType;
332: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
333: 					  }>
334: 					| undefined,
335: 			}));
336: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
337: 		} catch {
338: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
339: 		}
340: 	});
341: 
342: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
343: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
344: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
345: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
346: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
347: 
348: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
349: 		wsHandler,
350: 		client,
351: 		sessionMgr,
352: 		messageCache,
353: 		permissionBridge,
354: 		questionBridge,
355: 		overrides,
356: 		ptyManager,
357: 		config,
358: 		log,
359: 		connectPtyUpstream,
360: 	};
361: 
362: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
363: 
364: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
365: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
366: 			try {
367: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
368: 			} catch (err) {
369: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
370: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
371: 					clientId,
372: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
373: 				);
374: 			}
375: 		});
376: 	});
377: 
378: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
379: 
380: 	wireSSEConsumer(
381: 		{
382: 			translator,
383: 			sessionMgr,
384: 			messageCache,
385: 			permissionBridge,
386: 			questionBridge,
387: 			overrides,
388: 			wsHandler,
389: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
390: 			log,
391: 		},
392: 		sseConsumer,
393: 	);
394: 
395: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
396: 
397: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
398: 
399: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
400: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
401: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
402: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
403: 				type: "permission_resolved",
404: 				requestId: id,
405: 				decision: "timeout",
406: 			});
407: 		}
408: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
409: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
410: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
411: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
412: 		}
413: 	}, 30_000);
414: 
415: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
416: 	if (
417: 		timeoutTimer &&
418: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
419: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
420: 	) {
421: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
422: 	}
423: 
424: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
425: 
426: 	return {
427: 		wsHandler,
428: 		sseConsumer,
429: 		client,
430: 		sessionMgr,
431: 		translator,
432: 		permissionBridge,
433: 		questionBridge,
434: 
435: 		async stop() {
436: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
437: 			overrides.dispose();
438: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
439: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
440: 			wsHandler.close();
441: 		},
442: 	};
443: }
444: 
445: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
446: 
447: /**
448:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
449:  *
450:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
451:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
452:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
453:  */
454: export async function createRelayStack(
455: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
456: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
457: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
458: 
459: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
460: 
461: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
462: 	if (!pushMgr) {
463: 		try {
464: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
465: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
466: 			await pushMgr.init();
467: 		} catch {
468: 			pushMgr = undefined;
469: 		}
470: 	}
471: 
472: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
473: 
474: 	const server = new RelayServer({
475: 		port: config.port,
476: 		host: config.host,
477: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
478: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
479: 		tls: config.tls,
480: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
481: 	});
482: 
483: 	server.addProject({
484: 		slug: config.slug,
485: 		directory: config.projectDir,
486: 		title: config.slug,
487: 	});
488: 
489: 	await server.start();
490: 
491: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
492: 	if (!maybeServer) {
493: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
494: 	}
495: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
496: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
497: 
498: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
499: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
500: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
501: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
502: 
503: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
504: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
505: 
506: 	const getProjectList = () =>
507: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
508: 			slug: p.slug,
509: 			title: p.title,
510: 			directory: p.directory,
511: 		}));
512: 
513: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
514: 	async function addProjectRelay(
515: 		directory: string,
516: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
517: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
518: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
519: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
520: 		}
521: 		directory = resolve(directory);
522: 
523: 		// Check if directory is already registered
524: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
525: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
526: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
527: 			}
528: 		}
529: 
530: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
531: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
532: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
533: 			throw new Error(
534: 				dirStat
535: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
536: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
537: 			);
538: 		}
539: 
540: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
541: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
542: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
543: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
544: 
545: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
546: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
547: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
548: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
549: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
550: 		}
551: 
552: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
553: 		try {
554: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
555: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
556: 				httpServer,
557: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
558: 				projectDir: directory,
559: 				slug,
560: 				noServer: true,
561: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
562: 				log,
563: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
564: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
565: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
566: 			});
567: 
568: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
569: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
570: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
571: 
572: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
573: 		} catch (err) {
574: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
575: 			relays.delete(slug);
576: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
577: 			throw err;
578: 		} finally {
579: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
580: 		}
581: 
582: 		return { slug, title, directory };
583: 	}
584: 
585: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
586: 
587: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
588: 		httpServer,
589: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
590: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
591: 		slug: config.slug,
592: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
593: 		log,
594: 		noServer: true,
595: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
596: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
597: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
598: 	});
599: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
600: 
601: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
602: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
603: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
604: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
605: 
606: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
607: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
608: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
609: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
610: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
611: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
612: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
613: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
614: 				: false;
615: 			if (!cookieOk) {
616: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
617: 				const pinOk =
618: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
619: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
620: 				if (!pinOk) {
621: 					socket.destroy();
622: 					return;
623: 				}
624: 			}
625: 		}
626: 
627: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
628: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
629: 		if (projectMatch) {
630: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
631: 			if (target) {
632: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
633: 			} else {
634: 				socket.destroy();
635: 			}
636: 			return;
637: 		}
638: 
639: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
640: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
641: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
642: 			return;
643: 		}
644: 
645: 		socket.destroy();
646: 	});
647: 
648: 	const urls = server.getUrls();
649: 	log(`   ✓ Server listening: ${urls.local}`);
650: 
651: 	return {
652: 		server,
653: 		wsHandler: relay.wsHandler,
654: 		sseConsumer: relay.sseConsumer,
655: 		client: relay.client,
656: 		sessionMgr: relay.sessionMgr,
657: 		translator: relay.translator,
658: 		permissionBridge: relay.permissionBridge,
659: 		questionBridge: relay.questionBridge,
660: 
661: 		getPort() {
662: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
663: 			if (typeof addr === "object" && addr) return addr.port;
664: 			return config.port;
665: 		},
666: 
667: 		getBaseUrl() {
668: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
669: 			const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : config.port;
670: 			const protocol = config.tls ? "https" : "http";
671: 			return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:${port}`;
672: 		},
673: 
674: 		async stop() {
675: 			for (const r of relays.values()) {
676: 				try {
677: 					await r.stop();
678: 				} catch {
679: 					/* ignore */
680: 				}
681: 			}
682: 			relays.clear();
683: 			await server.stop();
684: 		},
685: 	};
686: }

2: // I/O layer: actual WebSocket server using the `ws` library.
3: // Wires ws-router.ts (pure logic) to real WebSocket connections.
4: 
5: import { randomBytes } from "node:crypto";
6: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
7: import type { IncomingMessage, Server } from "node:http";
8: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
9: import type { Duplex } from "node:stream";
10: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
11: import {
12: 	type ClientTracker,
13: 	createClientCountMessage,
14: 	createClientTracker,
15: 	type IncomingMessageType,
16: 	isRouteError,
17: 	parseIncomingMessage,
18: 	routeMessage,
19: } from "./ws-router.js";
20: 
21: // Use createRequire to import ws — the ws package is CJS-only and
22: // named ESM imports behave inconsistently across tsx, vitest, and Node ESM.
23: const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
24: const ws = require("ws");
25: const WebSocketServerClass =
26: 	ws.WebSocketServer as typeof import("ws").WebSocketServer;
27: type WSType = import("ws").WebSocket;
28: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
29: 
30: const WS_OPEN = 1; // WebSocket.OPEN constant
31: 
32: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33: 
34: export interface WebSocketHandlerOptions {
35: 	/** Path prefix for WebSocket upgrades (e.g., "/p/my-project") */
36: 	pathPrefix?: string;
37: 	/** Maximum message size in bytes */
38: 	maxPayload?: number;
39: 	/** Heartbeat interval in ms */
40: 	heartbeatInterval?: number;
41: 	/** Optional auth check on WebSocket upgrade (ws library verifyClient). */
42: 	verifyClient?: (
43: 		info: {
44: 			origin: string;
45: 			secure: boolean;
46: 			req: import("node:http").IncomingMessage;
47: 		},
48: 		callback: (result: boolean, code?: number, message?: string) => void,
49: 	) => void;
50: }
51: 
52: export interface WebSocketHandlerEvents {
53: 	/** Client connected */
54: 	client_connected: [{ clientId: string; clientCount: number }];
55: 	/** Client disconnected */
56: 	client_disconnected: [{ clientId: string; clientCount: number }];
57: 	/** Incoming message from a client */
58: 	message: [
59: 		{
60: 			clientId: string;
61: 			handler: IncomingMessageType;
62: 			payload: Record<string, unknown>;
63: 		},
64: 	];
65: 	/** Error on a client connection */
66: 	client_error: [{ clientId: string; error: Error }];
67: }
68: 
69: // ─── WebSocket Handler ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70: 
71: export class WebSocketHandler extends EventEmitter<WebSocketHandlerEvents> {
72: 	private readonly wss: InstanceType<typeof WebSocketServerClass>;
73: 	private readonly clients: Map<string, WSType> = new Map();
74: 	private readonly tracker: ClientTracker;
75: 	private heartbeatTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
76: 	private readonly heartbeatInterval: number;
77: 
78: 	constructor(server: Server | null, options: WebSocketHandlerOptions = {}) {
79: 		super();
80: 		this.tracker = createClientTracker();
81: 		this.heartbeatInterval = options.heartbeatInterval ?? 30_000;
82: 
83: 		// When server is null, create in noServer mode — the caller (daemon)
84: 		// handles HTTP upgrades and calls handleUpgrade() directly.
85: 		// Do NOT pass { server: null } to ws — it throws.
86: 		const wssOptions = server
87: 			? {
88: 					server,
89: 					path: options.pathPrefix ? `${options.pathPrefix}/ws` : undefined,
90: 					// verifyClient runs during the auto-attached upgrade handler
91: 					verifyClient: options.verifyClient,
92: 				}
93: 			: {
94: 					noServer: true as const,
95: 					// In noServer mode, ws still calls verifyClient during handleUpgrade.
96: 					// But the daemon's upgrade handler already checks auth, so skip it
97: 					// to avoid redundant double-checking.
98: 				};
99: 
100: 		this.wss = new WebSocketServerClass({
101: 			...wssOptions,
102: 			maxPayload: options.maxPayload ?? 1024 * 1024, // 1MB default
103: 		});
104: 
105: 		this.wss.on("connection", (wsConn: WSType, req: IncomingMessage) =>
106: 			this.onConnection(wsConn, req),
107: 		);
108: 
109: 		// Start heartbeat
110: 		this.startHeartbeat();
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	/** Broadcast a message to all connected clients */
114: 	broadcast(message: RelayMessage): void {
115: 		const data = JSON.stringify(message);
116: 		for (const [_id, wsConn] of this.clients) {
117: 			if (wsConn.readyState === WS_OPEN) {
118: 				wsConn.send(data);
119: 			}
120: 		}
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	/** Send a message to all clients except one (e.g., the sender) */
124: 	broadcastExcept(message: RelayMessage, excludeClientId: string): void {
125: 		const data = JSON.stringify(message);
126: 		for (const [id, wsConn] of this.clients) {
127: 			if (id !== excludeClientId && wsConn.readyState === WS_OPEN) {
128: 				wsConn.send(data);
129: 			}
130: 		}
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	/** Send a message to a specific client */
134: 	sendTo(clientId: string, message: RelayMessage): void {
135: 		const wsConn = this.clients.get(clientId);
136: 		if (wsConn && wsConn.readyState === WS_OPEN) {
137: 			wsConn.send(JSON.stringify(message));
138: 		}
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	/** Get connected client count */
142: 	getClientCount(): number {
143: 		return this.tracker.getClientCount();
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	/** Get all client IDs */
147: 	getClientIds(): string[] {
148: 		return this.tracker.getClientIds();
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	/** Close all connections and clean up */
152: 	close(): void {
153: 		if (this.heartbeatTimer) {
154: 			clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimer);
155: 			this.heartbeatTimer = null;
156: 		}
157: 
158: 		for (const [_id, wsConn] of this.clients) {
159: 			wsConn.close(1001, "Server shutting down");
160: 		}
161: 
162: 		this.clients.clear();
163: 		this.wss.close();
164: 	}
165: 
166: 	/**
167: 	 * Handle an HTTP upgrade manually (for noServer mode).
168: 	 * The daemon's upgrade handler calls this after routing by slug and checking auth.
169: 	 */
170: 	handleUpgrade(req: IncomingMessage, socket: Duplex, head: Buffer): void {
171: 		this.wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
172: 			this.wss.emit("connection", ws, req);
173: 		});
174: 	}
175: 
176: 	// ─── Internal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: 
178: 	private onConnection(wsConn: WSType, _req: IncomingMessage): void {
179: 		const clientId = randomBytes(8).toString("hex");
180: 		this.clients.set(clientId, wsConn);
181: 		const count = this.tracker.addClient(clientId);
182: 
183: 		// Notify about new client
184: 		this.emit("client_connected", { clientId, clientCount: count });
185: 
186: 		// Send client count update to all
187: 		this.broadcast(createClientCountMessage(count));
188: 
189: 		// Handle messages
190: 		wsConn.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
191: 			this.onMessage(clientId, data);
192: 		});
193: 
194: 		// Handle close
195: 		wsConn.on("close", () => {
196: 			this.clients.delete(clientId);
197: 			const newCount = this.tracker.removeClient(clientId);
198: 			this.emit("client_disconnected", { clientId, clientCount: newCount });
199: 			this.broadcast(createClientCountMessage(newCount));
200: 		});
201: 
202: 		// Handle errors
203: 		wsConn.on("error", (err: Error) => {
204: 			this.emit("client_error", { clientId, error: err });
205: 		});
206: 
207: 		// Mark as alive for heartbeat
208: 		(wsConn as WSType & { isAlive: boolean }).isAlive = true;
209: 		wsConn.on("pong", () => {
210: 			(wsConn as WSType & { isAlive: boolean }).isAlive = true;
211: 		});
212: 	}
213: 
214: 	private onMessage(clientId: string, raw: RawData): void {
215: 		const str = raw.toString();
216: 		const parsed = parseIncomingMessage(str);
217: 
218: 		if (!parsed) {
219: 			// Invalid JSON — send error but don't disconnect (AC7)
220: 			this.sendTo(clientId, {
221: 				type: "error",
222: 				code: "PARSE_ERROR",
223: 				message: "Could not parse message as JSON",
224: 			});
225: 			return;
226: 		}
227: 
228: 		const result = routeMessage(parsed);
229: 
230: 		if (isRouteError(result)) {
231: 			// Unknown message type — send error but don't disconnect (AC7)
232: 			this.sendTo(clientId, {
233: 				type: "error",
234: 				code: result.code,
235: 				message: result.message,
236: 			});
237: 			return;
238: 		}
239: 
240: 		// Emit for the project context to handle
241: 		this.emit("message", {
242: 			clientId,
243: 			handler: result.handler,
244: 			payload: result.payload,
245: 		});
246: 	}
247: 
248: 	private startHeartbeat(): void {
249: 		this.heartbeatTimer = setInterval(() => {
250: 			for (const [clientId, wsConn] of this.clients) {
251: 				const aliveWs = wsConn as WSType & { isAlive: boolean };
252: 				if (!aliveWs.isAlive) {
253: 					// Dead connection — terminate
254: 					this.clients.delete(clientId);
255: 					const count = this.tracker.removeClient(clientId);
256: 					this.emit("client_disconnected", { clientId, clientCount: count });
257: 					this.broadcast(createClientCountMessage(count));
258: 					wsConn.terminate();
259: 					continue;
260: 				}
261: 				aliveWs.isAlive = false;
262: 				wsConn.ping();
263: 			}
264: 		}, this.heartbeatInterval);
265: 	}
266: }

<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Message Router (Ticket 2.2) ──────────────────────────────────
2: // Pure logic for routing incoming WebSocket messages to correct handlers,
3: // client tracking, broadcast targeting, and state snapshot building.
4: // Deliberately IO-free: no actual WebSocket I/O — just routing decisions.
5: 
6: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
7: 
8: // ─── Message types from browser ──────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export type IncomingMessageType =
11: 	| "message"
12: 	| "permission_response"
13: 	| "ask_user_response"
14: 	| "question_reject"
15: 	| "new_session"
16: 	| "switch_session"
17: 	| "delete_session"
18: 	| "rename_session"
19: 	| "list_sessions"
20: 	| "search_sessions"
21: 	| "load_more_history"
22: 	| "terminal_command"
23: 	| "input_sync"
24: 	| "switch_agent"
25: 	| "switch_model"
26: 	| "get_todo"
27: 	| "get_agents"
28: 	| "get_models"
29: 	| "get_commands"
30: 	| "get_projects"
31: 	| "add_project"
32: 	| "get_file_list"
33: 	| "get_file_content"
34: 	| "pty_create"
35: 	| "pty_input"
36: 	| "pty_resize"
37: 	| "pty_close"
38: 	| "cancel"
39: 	| "rewind";
40: 
41: const VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES = new Set<string>([
42: 	"message",
43: 	"permission_response",
44: 	"ask_user_response",
45: 	"question_reject",
46: 	"new_session",
47: 	"switch_session",
48: 	"delete_session",
49: 	"rename_session",
50: 	"list_sessions",
51: 	"search_sessions",
52: 	"load_more_history",
53: 	"terminal_command",
54: 	"input_sync",
55: 	"switch_agent",
56: 	"switch_model",
57: 	"get_todo",
58: 	"get_agents",
59: 	"get_models",
60: 	"get_commands",
61: 	"get_projects",
62: 	"add_project",
63: 	"get_file_list",
64: 	"get_file_content",
65: 	"pty_create",
66: 	"pty_input",
67: 	"pty_resize",
68: 	"pty_close",
69: 	"cancel",
70: 	"rewind",
71: ]);
72: 
73: export interface IncomingMessage {
74: 	type: string;
75: 	[key: string]: unknown;
76: }
77: 
78: export interface RouteResult {
79: 	handler: IncomingMessageType;
80: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>;
81: }
82: 
83: export interface ErrorResult {
84: 	type: "error";
85: 	code: string;
86: 	message: string;
87: }
88: 
89: // ─── Message routing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: 
91: /**
92:  * Parse and validate an incoming WebSocket message.
93:  * Returns the parsed object or null if parsing fails.
94:  */
95: export function parseIncomingMessage(raw: string): IncomingMessage | null {
96: 	try {
97: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
98: 		if (
99: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
100: 			parsed === null ||
101: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
102: 		) {
103: 			return null;
104: 		}
105: 		if (typeof parsed.type !== "string") {
106: 			return null;
107: 		}
108: 		return parsed as IncomingMessage;
109: 	} catch {
110: 		return null;
111: 	}
112: }
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Route a parsed message to its handler.
116:  * Returns either a RouteResult or an ErrorResult for unknown types.
117:  */
118: export function routeMessage(msg: IncomingMessage): RouteResult | ErrorResult {
119: 	if (!VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES.has(msg.type)) {
120: 		return {
121: 			type: "error",
122: 			code: "UNKNOWN_MESSAGE_TYPE",
123: 			message: `Unknown message type: ${msg.type}`,
124: 		};
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	const { type, ...payload } = msg;
128: 	return {
129: 		handler: type as IncomingMessageType,
130: 		payload,
131: 	};
132: }
133: 
134: /**
135:  * Check if a route result is an error.
136:  */
137: export function isRouteError(
138: 	result: RouteResult | ErrorResult,
139: ): result is ErrorResult {
140: 	return "type" in result && (result as ErrorResult).type === "error";
141: }
142: 
143: // ─── Client tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144: 
145: export interface ClientTracker {
146: 	addClient(clientId: string): number;
147: 	removeClient(clientId: string): number;
148: 	getClientCount(): number;
149: 	getClientIds(): string[];
150: 	hasClient(clientId: string): boolean;
151: 	getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[];
152: }
153: 
154: /**
155:  * Create a client tracker for managing connected WebSocket clients.
156:  */
157: export function createClientTracker(): ClientTracker {
158: 	const clients = new Set<string>();
159: 
160: 	return {
161: 		addClient(clientId: string): number {
162: 			clients.add(clientId);
163: 			return clients.size;
164: 		},
165: 
166: 		removeClient(clientId: string): number {
167: 			clients.delete(clientId);
168: 			return clients.size;
169: 		},
170: 
171: 		getClientCount(): number {
172: 			return clients.size;
173: 		},
174: 
175: 		getClientIds(): string[] {
176: 			return [...clients];
177: 		},
178: 
179: 		hasClient(clientId: string): boolean {
180: 			return clients.has(clientId);
181: 		},
182: 
183: 		getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[] {
184: 			if (!excludeClientId) return [...clients];
185: 			return [...clients].filter((id) => id !== excludeClientId);
186: 		},
187: 	};
188: }
189: 
190: // ─── State snapshot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191: 
192: export interface SessionState {
193: 	sessionId: string;
194: 	title: string;
195: 	model?: string;
196: 	provider?: string;
197: 	status: "idle" | "processing";
198: 	clientCount: number;
199: }
200: 
201: export interface PendingState {
202: 	permissions: Array<{ requestId: string; toolName: string }>;
203: 	questions: Array<{ toolId: string; questions: unknown[] }>;
204: }
205: 
206: export interface StateSnapshot {
207: 	session: SessionState;
208: 	pending: PendingState;
209: 	clientCount: number;
210: }
211: 
212: /**
213:  * Build a state snapshot for a newly connected client.
214:  * The snapshot includes the session state, pending interactions, and client count.
215:  */
216: export function buildStateSnapshot(
217: 	session: SessionState,
218: 	pending: PendingState,
219: 	clientCount: number,
220: ): StateSnapshot {
221: 	return {
222: 		session: { ...session, clientCount },
223: 		pending: {
224: 			permissions: [...pending.permissions],
225: 			questions: [...pending.questions],
226: 		},
227: 		clientCount,
228: 	};
229: }
230: 
231: /**
232:  * Build the list of messages to send to a newly connected client.
233:  * Returns RelayMessage[] in the correct order per AC5.
234:  */
235: export function buildNewClientMessages(
236: 	snapshot: StateSnapshot,
237: ): RelayMessage[] {
238: 	const messages: RelayMessage[] = [];
239: 
240: 	// 1. Status
241: 	messages.push({
242: 		type: "status",
243: 		status: snapshot.session.status,
244: 	});
245: 
246: 	// 2. Client count
247: 	messages.push({
248: 		type: "client_count",
249: 		count: snapshot.clientCount,
250: 	});
251: 
252: 	// 3. Pending permissions
253: 	for (const perm of snapshot.pending.permissions) {
254: 		messages.push({
255: 			type: "permission_request",
256: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
257: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
258: 			toolInput: {},
259: 		});
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	// 4. Pending questions
263: 	for (const q of snapshot.pending.questions) {
264: 		messages.push({
265: 			type: "ask_user",
266: 			toolId: q.toolId,
267: 			questions: q.questions as RelayMessage extends { type: "ask_user" }
268: 				? never
269: 				: unknown[],
270: 		} as RelayMessage);
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	return messages;
274: }
275: 
276: // ─── Client count message factory ────────────────────────────────────────────
277: 
278: export function createClientCountMessage(count: number): RelayMessage {
279: 	return { type: "client_count", count };
280: }

<content>1: // ─── Client Init (Ticket 3.6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Handles the initial handshake when a browser client connects via WebSocket.
3: // Sends session info (with cached events or REST API history), model info,
4: // agent list, provider/model list, and PTY replay to the new client.
5: //
6: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts's `client_connected` handler so the logic is
7: // independently testable and relay-stack stays slim.
8: 
9: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
10: import { filterAgents } from "./handlers/index.js";
11: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
12: import type { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
13: import type { PtyManager } from "./pty-manager.js";
14: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
15: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
16: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
17: 
18: // ─── Dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: 
20: export interface ClientInitDeps {
21: 	wsHandler: {
22: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
23: 		sendTo: (clientId: string, msg: RelayMessage) => void;
24: 	};
25: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
26: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
27: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
28: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
29: 	ptyManager: PtyManager;
30: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
31: }
32: 
33: // ─── Handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34: 
35: /**
36:  * Handle a newly connected browser client. Sends all initial state:
37:  * - Active session with cached events or REST API history
38:  * - Session list
39:  * - Model info (from session or overrides)
40:  * - Agent list (filtered)
41:  * - Provider/model list (connected only)
42:  * - PTY list + scrollback replay
43:  *
44:  * Errors are sent as INIT_FAILED messages without crashing the handler.
45:  */
46: export async function handleClientConnected(
47: 	deps: ClientInitDeps,
48: 	clientId: string,
49: ): Promise<void> {
50: 	const { wsHandler, client, sessionMgr, messageCache, overrides, ptyManager } =
51: 		deps;
52: 
53: 	const sendInitError = (err: unknown, prefix: string) => {
54: 		deps.log(`   [ws] ${prefix}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
55: 		wsHandler.sendTo(
56: 			clientId,
57: 			RelayError.fromCaught(err, "INIT_FAILED", prefix).toMessage(),
58: 		);
59: 	};
60: 
61: 	// ── Active session with event replay ─────────────────────────────────
62: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
63: 	if (activeId) {
64: 		const events = messageCache.getEvents(activeId);
65: 		const hasChatContent =
66: 			events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
67: 			false;
68: 		if (events && hasChatContent) {
69: 			// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
70: 			wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
71: 				type: "session_switched",
72: 				id: activeId,
73: 				events,
74: 			});
75: 		} else {
76: 			// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
77: 			try {
78: 				const history = await sessionMgr.loadHistory(activeId);
79: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
80: 					type: "session_switched",
81: 					id: activeId,
82: 					history: {
83: 						messages: history.messages,
84: 						hasMore: history.hasMore,
85: 						total: history.total,
86: 					},
87: 				});
88: 			} catch {
89: 				// If REST API fails too, just send the switch without history
90: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
91: 					type: "session_switched",
92: 					id: activeId,
93: 				});
94: 			}
95: 		}
96: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "status", status: "idle" });
97: 
98: 		// Send model/agent info from the active session
99: 		try {
100: 			const session = await client.getSession(activeId);
101: 			if (session.modelID) {
102: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
103: 					type: "model_info",
104: 					model: session.modelID,
105: 					provider: session.providerID ?? "",
106: 				});
107: 			} else if (overrides.model) {
108: 				// Session has no model set, but relay has a selected model
109: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
110: 					type: "model_info",
111: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
112: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
113: 				});
114: 			}
115: 		} catch (err) {
116: 			sendInitError(err, "Failed to load session info");
117: 			if (overrides.model) {
118: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
119: 					type: "model_info",
120: 					model: overrides.model.modelID,
121: 					provider: overrides.model.providerID,
122: 				});
123: 			}
124: 		}
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// ── Session list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128: 	try {
129: 		const sessions = await sessionMgr.listSessions();
130: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "session_list", sessions });
131: 	} catch (err) {
132: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list sessions");
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// ── Agent list (filter out internal agents) ──────────────────────────
136: 	try {
137: 		const rawAgents = await client.listAgents();
138: 		const agents = filterAgents(rawAgents);
139: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "agent_list", agents });
140: 	} catch (err) {
141: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list agents");
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	// ── Provider/model list + auto-select default ────────────────────────
145: 	try {
146: 		const providerResult = await client.listProviders();
147: 		const connectedSet = new Set(providerResult.connected);
148: 		const providers = providerResult.providers
149: 			.map((p) => ({
150: 				id: p.id || p.name || "",
151: 				name: p.name || p.id || "",
152: 				configured: connectedSet.has(p.id) || connectedSet.has(p.name),
153: 				models: (p.models ?? []).map((m) => ({
154: 					id: m.id,
155: 					name: m.name || m.id,
156: 					provider: p.id || p.name || "",
157: 				})),
158: 			}))
159: 			.filter((p) => p.configured);
160: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "model_list", providers });
161: 
162: 		// Auto-select default model from a connected provider if none set
163: 		if (!overrides.model) {
164: 			for (const providerId of providerResult.connected) {
165: 				const defaultModelId = providerResult.defaults[providerId];
166: 				if (defaultModelId) {
167: 					overrides.setModelDefault({
168: 						providerID: providerId,
169: 						modelID: defaultModelId,
170: 					});
171: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
172: 						type: "model_info",
173: 						model: defaultModelId,
174: 						provider: providerId,
175: 					});
176: 					deps.log(
177: 						`   [model] Auto-selected default: ${defaultModelId} (${providerId})`,
178: 					);
179: 					break;
180: 				}
181: 			}
182: 		}
183: 	} catch (err) {
184: 		sendInitError(err, "Failed to list providers");
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	// ── PTY list + scrollback replay ─────────────────────────────────────
188: 	if (ptyManager.sessionCount > 0) {
189: 		const ptys = ptyManager.listSessions();
190: 		wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, { type: "pty_list", ptys });
191: 		// Replay scrollback for each PTY to this specific client
192: 		for (const { id: ptyId } of ptys) {
193: 			const scrollback = ptyManager.getScrollback(ptyId);
194: 			if (scrollback) {
195: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
196: 					type: "pty_output",
197: 					ptyId,
198: 					data: scrollback,
199: 				});
200: 			}
201: 			const session = ptyManager.getSession(ptyId);
202: 			if (session?.exited) {
203: 				wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
204: 					type: "pty_exited",
205: 					ptyId,
206: 					exitCode: session.exitCode ?? 0,
207: 				});
208: 			}
209: 		}
210: 	}
211: }

<content>1: // ─── Per-Session Message Cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Records every translated event per session in real-time (memory + JSONL file),
3: // replays on session switch through existing client handlers.
4: //
5: // Modeled after claude-relay's doSendAndRecord + appendToSessionFile pattern:
6: //   - Record synchronously before broadcast (identical to claude-relay)
7: //   - Append-only JSONL files (crash-safe, O(1) per event)
8: //   - Fallback chain: memory → file → null (caller uses REST API)
9: 
10: import {
11: 	appendFileSync,
12: 	mkdirSync,
13: 	readdirSync,
14: 	readFileSync,
15: 	unlinkSync,
16: 	writeFileSync,
17: } from "node:fs";
18: import { join } from "node:path";
19: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
20: 
21: /** Maximum events per session before eviction. */
22: const MAX_EVENTS = 5000;
23: 
24: /** After eviction, keep this fraction of events (newest). */
25: const KEEP_RATIO = 0.8;
26: 
27: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: export interface FileOpResult {
30: 	ok: boolean;
31: 	error?: string;
32: }
33: 
34: interface SessionCache {
35: 	events: RelayMessage[];
36: }
37: 
38: // ─── MessageCache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: 
40: export class MessageCache {
41: 	private readonly sessions = new Map<string, SessionCache>();
42: 	private readonly cacheDir: string;
43: 
44: 	constructor(cacheDir: string) {
45: 		this.cacheDir = cacheDir;
46: 		mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true });
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	// ── Recording (real-time, identical to doSendAndRecord) ────────────
50: 
51: 	/**
52: 	 * Append event to memory + file. Called synchronously before broadcast.
53: 	 * Identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord pattern.
54: 	 * Evicts oldest events when MAX_EVENTS is exceeded.
55: 	 */
56: 	recordEvent(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): void {
57: 		const session = this.ensureSession(sessionId);
58: 		session.events.push(event);
59: 		this.appendToFile(sessionId, event);
60: 
61: 		// Evict oldest events when over limit
62: 		if (session.events.length > MAX_EVENTS) {
63: 			const keepCount = Math.floor(MAX_EVENTS * KEEP_RATIO);
64: 			session.events = session.events.slice(-keepCount);
65: 			this.rewriteFile(sessionId, session.events);
66: 		}
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	// ── Serving (fallback chain) ───────────────────────────────────────
70: 
71: 	/**
72: 	 * Get raw events for a session. Fallback chain:
73: 	 *   1. In-memory events → return if found
74: 	 *   2. Load from JSONL file on disk → return if found
75: 	 *   3. Return null → caller fetches from OpenCode REST API
76: 	 */
77: 	getEvents(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
78: 		// 1. In-memory
79: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
80: 		if (session && session.events.length > 0) return session.events;
81: 
82: 		// 2. File on disk
83: 		const fromFile = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
84: 		if (fromFile && fromFile.length > 0) {
85: 			this.sessions.set(sessionId, { events: fromFile });
86: 			return fromFile;
87: 		}
88: 
89: 		// 3. Return null → caller fetches from REST API
90: 		return null;
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// ── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94: 
95: 	/** Remove all data (memory + file). Called on session delete. */
96: 	remove(sessionId: string): void {
97: 		this.sessions.delete(sessionId);
98: 		try {
99: 			unlinkSync(this.filePath(sessionId));
100: 		} catch {
101: 			// File may not exist — that's fine
102: 		}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	/** Load all .jsonl files into memory. Called once on startup. */
106: 	loadFromDisk(): void {
107: 		let files: string[];
108: 		try {
109: 			files = readdirSync(this.cacheDir);
110: 		} catch {
111: 			return;
112: 		}
113: 
114: 		for (const file of files) {
115: 			if (!file.endsWith(".jsonl")) continue;
116: 			const sessionId = file.slice(0, -6); // Remove ".jsonl"
117: 			const events = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
118: 			if (events && events.length > 0) {
119: 				this.sessions.set(sessionId, { events });
120: 			}
121: 		}
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check if a session has cached events in memory. */
125: 	has(sessionId: string): boolean {
126: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
127: 		return session !== undefined && session.events.length > 0;
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	/** Number of sessions currently cached. */
131: 	sessionCount(): number {
132: 		return this.sessions.size;
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// ── Internal ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: 
137: 	private ensureSession(sessionId: string): SessionCache {
138: 		let session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
139: 		if (!session) {
140: 			session = { events: [] };
141: 			this.sessions.set(sessionId, session);
142: 		}
143: 		return session;
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	private filePath(sessionId: string): string {
147: 		return join(this.cacheDir, `${sessionId}.jsonl`);
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	private loadFromFile(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
151: 		let content: string;
152: 		try {
153: 			content = readFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), "utf8");
154: 		} catch {
155: 			return null;
156: 		}
157: 
158: 		const lines = content.trim().split("\n");
159: 		if (lines.length === 0 || (lines.length === 1 && lines[0] === "")) {
160: 			return null;
161: 		}
162: 
163: 		const events: RelayMessage[] = [];
164: 		for (const line of lines) {
165: 			if (!line) continue;
166: 			try {
167: 				events.push(JSON.parse(line) as RelayMessage);
168: 			} catch {
169: 				// Skip malformed lines (crash-safe: partial last line is OK)
170: 			}
171: 		}
172: 		return events.length > 0 ? events : null;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	private appendToFile(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): FileOpResult {
176: 		try {
177: 			appendFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), `${JSON.stringify(event)}\n`);
178: 			return { ok: true };
179: 		} catch (err) {
180: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	/** Rewrite the JSONL file with only the given events (after eviction). */
185: 	private rewriteFile(sessionId: string, events: RelayMessage[]): FileOpResult {
186: 		try {
187: 			const content = `${events.map((e) => JSON.stringify(e)).join("\n")}\n`;
188: 			writeFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), content);
189: 			return { ok: true };
190: 		} catch (err) {
191: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: }

2: 
3: import type {
4: 	FrontendDecision,
5: 	OpenCodeDecision,
6: 	OpenCodeEvent,
7: 	PendingPermission,
8: } from "./types.js";
9: 
10: // ─── Decision Mapping ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: const DECISION_MAP: Record<FrontendDecision, OpenCodeDecision> = {
13: 	allow: "once",
14: 	deny: "reject",
15: 	allow_always: "always",
16: };
17: 
18: const REVERSE_DECISION_MAP: Record<OpenCodeDecision, FrontendDecision> = {
19: 	once: "allow",
20: 	reject: "deny",
21: 	always: "allow_always",
22: };
23: 
24: /** Map frontend decision vocabulary → OpenCode vocabulary */
25: export function mapDecision(frontend: string): OpenCodeDecision | null {
26: 	return DECISION_MAP[frontend as FrontendDecision] ?? null;
27: }
28: 
29: /** Map OpenCode decision vocabulary → frontend vocabulary */
30: export function mapDecisionReverse(opencode: string): FrontendDecision | null {
31: 	return REVERSE_DECISION_MAP[opencode as OpenCodeDecision] ?? null;
32: }
33: 
34: // ─── Permission Bridge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: export interface PermissionBridgeOptions {
37: 	timeoutMs?: number;
38: 	now?: () => number;
39: }
40: 
41: export class PermissionBridge {
42: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingPermission> = new Map();
43: 	private alwaysAllowed: Set<string> = new Set();
44: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
45: 	private readonly now: () => number;
46: 
47: 	constructor(options: PermissionBridgeOptions = {}) {
48: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 5 * 60_000; // 5 minutes
49: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	/** Process an incoming permission.asked SSE event */
53: 	onPermissionRequest(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingPermission | null {
54: 		const props = event.properties as {
55: 			id?: string;
56: 			permission?: string;
57: 			patterns?: string[];
58: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
59: 			always?: string[];
60: 		};
61: 
62: 		if (!props.id || !props.permission) return null;
63: 
64: 		// Auto-approve if tool is in the always-allowed set
65: 		if (this.alwaysAllowed.has(props.permission)) {
66: 			return null; // Caller should auto-reply
67: 		}
68: 
69: 		const entry: PendingPermission = {
70: 			requestId: props.id,
71: 			toolName: props.permission,
72: 			toolInput: {
73: 				patterns: props.patterns ?? [],
74: 				metadata: props.metadata ?? {},
75: 			},
76: 			always: props.always ?? [],
77: 			timestamp: this.now(),
78: 		};
79: 
80: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
81: 		return entry;
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/** Process a user's permission decision from the browser */
85: 	onPermissionResponse(
86: 		requestId: string,
87: 		decision: string,
88: 	): { mapped: OpenCodeDecision; toolName: string } | null {
89: 		const entry = this.pending.get(requestId);
90: 		if (!entry) return null; // Already resolved or unknown — ignore duplicate
91: 
92: 		const mapped = mapDecision(decision);
93: 		if (!mapped) return null;
94: 
95: 		// Track "always" decisions
96: 		if (mapped === "always") {
97: 			this.alwaysAllowed.add(entry.toolName);
98: 		}
99: 
100: 		this.pending.delete(requestId);
101: 		return { mapped, toolName: entry.toolName };
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/** Handle a permission.replied SSE event (resolved from CLI) */
105: 	onPermissionReplied(requestId: string): boolean {
106: 		return this.pending.delete(requestId);
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	/** Get all pending permissions (for replay on reconnect) */
110: 	getPending(): PendingPermission[] {
111: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	/** Check if a tool is in the always-allowed set */
115: 	isAlwaysAllowed(toolName: string): boolean {
116: 		return this.alwaysAllowed.has(toolName);
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Clear the always-allowed cache */
120: 	clearAlwaysAllowed(): void {
121: 		this.alwaysAllowed.clear();
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check for timed-out permissions and return their IDs */
125: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
126: 		const now = this.now();
127: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
128: 
129: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
130: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
131: 				timedOut.push(id);
132: 				this.pending.delete(id);
133: 			}
134: 		}
135: 
136: 		return timedOut;
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	/** Recover pending permissions from REST API response */
140: 	recoverPending(
141: 		permissions: Array<{
142: 			id: string;
143: 			permission: string;
144: 			patterns?: string[];
145: 			metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
146: 			always?: string[];
147: 		}>,
148: 	): PendingPermission[] {
149: 		const recovered: PendingPermission[] = [];
150: 		for (const p of permissions) {
151: 			const entry: PendingPermission = {
152: 				requestId: p.id,
153: 				toolName: p.permission,
154: 				toolInput: {
155: 					patterns: p.patterns ?? [],
156: 					metadata: p.metadata ?? {},
157: 				},
158: 				always: p.always ?? [],
159: 				timestamp: this.now(),
160: 			};
161: 			this.pending.set(p.id, entry);
162: 			recovered.push(entry);
163: 		}
164: 		return recovered;
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	/** Number of pending permissions */
168: 	get size(): number {
169: 		return this.pending.size;
170: 	}
171: }

<content>1: // ─── SSE Reconnection & Backoff (Ticket 1.2) ────────────────────────────────
2: // Pure logic for exponential backoff calculation, connection health tracking,
3: // session ID filtering, and event parsing. Deliberately IO-free.
4: 
5: import type { ConnectionHealth, GlobalEvent, OpenCodeEvent } from "./types.js";
6: 
7: // ─── Exponential backoff ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: 
9: export interface BackoffConfig {
10: 	baseDelay: number; // Initial delay in ms (default: 1000)
11: 	maxDelay: number; // Maximum delay in ms (default: 30000)
12: 	multiplier: number; // Multiplier per attempt (default: 2)
13: }
14: 
15: const DEFAULT_BACKOFF_CONFIG: BackoffConfig = {
16: 	baseDelay: 1000,
17: 	maxDelay: 30000,
18: 	multiplier: 2,
19: };
20: 
21: /**
22:  * Calculate the next reconnection delay using exponential backoff.
23:  * delay = min(baseDelay * multiplier^attempt, maxDelay)
24:  * Always returns a value between baseDelay and maxDelay.
25:  */
26: export function calculateBackoffDelay(
27: 	attempt: number,
28: 	config: BackoffConfig = DEFAULT_BACKOFF_CONFIG,
29: ): number {
30: 	if (attempt < 0) return config.baseDelay;
31: 	const delay = config.baseDelay * config.multiplier ** attempt;
32: 	return Math.min(delay, config.maxDelay);
33: }
34: 
35: /**
36:  * Calculate the sequence of delays for N reconnection attempts.
37:  */
38: export function getBackoffSequence(
39: 	numAttempts: number,
40: 	config: BackoffConfig = DEFAULT_BACKOFF_CONFIG,
41: ): number[] {
42: 	const seq: number[] = [];
43: 	for (let i = 0; i < numAttempts; i++) {
44: 		seq.push(calculateBackoffDelay(i, config));
45: 	}
46: 	return seq;
47: }
48: 
49: // ─── Connection health tracking ──────────────────────────────────────────────
50: 
51: export interface HealthTracker {
52: 	onConnected(): void;
53: 	onDisconnected(): void;
54: 	onEvent(): void;
55: 	onReconnect(): void;
56: 	getHealth(): ConnectionHealth & { stale: boolean };
57: 	isStale(): boolean;
58: 	getReconnectCount(): number;
59: }
60: 
61: export interface HealthTrackerConfig {
62: 	staleThreshold: number; // ms — mark as stale if no event within this window
63: 	now?: () => number;
64: }
65: 
66: const DEFAULT_HEALTH_CONFIG: HealthTrackerConfig = {
67: 	staleThreshold: 60_000,
68: };
69: 
70: /**
71:  * Create a health tracker that monitors SSE connection state.
72:  */
73: export function createHealthTracker(
74: 	config: HealthTrackerConfig = DEFAULT_HEALTH_CONFIG,
75: ): HealthTracker {
76: 	const now = config.now ?? (() => Date.now());
77: 	let connected = false;
78: 	let lastEventAt: number | null = null;
79: 	let reconnectCount = 0;
80: 
81: 	return {
82: 		onConnected() {
83: 			connected = true;
84: 		},
85: 
86: 		onDisconnected() {
87: 			connected = false;
88: 		},
89: 
90: 		onEvent() {
91: 			lastEventAt = now();
92: 		},
93: 
94: 		onReconnect() {
95: 			reconnectCount++;
96: 			connected = true;
97: 		},
98: 
99: 		getHealth() {
100: 			return {
101: 				connected,
102: 				lastEventAt,
103: 				reconnectCount,
104: 				stale: this.isStale(),
105: 			};
106: 		},
107: 
108: 		isStale() {
109: 			if (!connected) return false;
110: 			if (lastEventAt === null) return false;
111: 			return now() - lastEventAt > config.staleThreshold;
112: 		},
113: 
114: 		getReconnectCount() {
115: 			return reconnectCount;
116: 		},
117: 	};
118: }
119: 
120: // ─── Session ID filtering ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
121: 
122: /**
123:  * Check if an event belongs to a given session.
124:  * Events without a sessionID are considered "global" and always pass.
125:  */
126: export function eventBelongsToSession(
127: 	event: OpenCodeEvent,
128: 	sessionId: string,
129: ): boolean {
130: 	const props = event.properties as { sessionID?: string };
131: 	// Events without sessionID are global — always pass
132: 	if (props.sessionID === undefined) return true;
133: 	return props.sessionID === sessionId;
134: }
135: 
136: /**
137:  * Filter a list of events to only those belonging to a given session.
138:  */
139: export function filterEventsBySession(
140: 	events: OpenCodeEvent[],
141: 	sessionId: string,
142: ): OpenCodeEvent[] {
143: 	return events.filter((e) => eventBelongsToSession(e, sessionId));
144: }
145: 
146: /**
147:  * Get the set of session IDs present in a list of events.
148:  */
149: export function getSessionIds(events: OpenCodeEvent[]): Set<string> {
150: 	const ids = new Set<string>();
151: 	for (const event of events) {
152: 		const props = event.properties as { sessionID?: string };
153: 		if (props.sessionID !== undefined) {
154: 			ids.add(props.sessionID);
155: 		}
156: 	}
157: 	return ids;
158: }
