160: // ─── Event parsing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161: 
162: export interface ParseResult {
163: 	ok: boolean;
164: 	event?: OpenCodeEvent;
165: 	error?: string;
166: }
167: 
168: /**
169:  * Parse a raw SSE data string into an OpenCodeEvent.
170:  * Never throws — returns { ok: false, error } for malformed data.
171:  */
172: export function parseSSEData(raw: string): ParseResult {
173: 	if (!raw || raw.trim() === "") {
174: 		return { ok: false, error: "empty data" };
175: 	}
176: 
177: 	try {
178: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
179: 		if (
180: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
181: 			parsed === null ||
182: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
183: 		) {
184: 			return { ok: false, error: "not an object" };
185: 		}
186: 		if (typeof parsed.type !== "string") {
187: 			return { ok: false, error: "missing or invalid type field" };
188: 		}
189: 		if (typeof parsed.properties !== "object" || parsed.properties === null) {
190: 			// Some events might not have properties — normalize
191: 			return {
192: 				ok: true,
193: 				event: { type: parsed.type, properties: parsed.properties ?? {} },
194: 			};
195: 		}
196: 		return { ok: true, event: parsed as OpenCodeEvent };
197: 	} catch (e) {
198: 		return { ok: false, error: `JSON parse error: ${(e as Error).message}` };
199: 	}
200: }
201: 
202: /**
203:  * Parse a raw SSE data string into a GlobalEvent.
204:  * Never throws — returns { ok: false, error } for malformed data.
205:  */
206: export function parseGlobalSSEData(raw: string): {
207: 	ok: boolean;
208: 	event?: GlobalEvent;
209: 	error?: string;
210: } {
211: 	if (!raw || raw.trim() === "") {
212: 		return { ok: false, error: "empty data" };
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	try {
216: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
217: 		if (
218: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
219: 			parsed === null ||
220: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
221: 		) {
222: 			return { ok: false, error: "not an object" };
223: 		}
224: 		if (typeof parsed.directory !== "string") {
225: 			return { ok: false, error: "missing directory field" };
226: 		}
227: 		if (typeof parsed.payload !== "object" || parsed.payload === null) {
228: 			return { ok: false, error: "missing or invalid payload" };
229: 		}
230: 		if (typeof parsed.payload.type !== "string") {
231: 			return { ok: false, error: "payload missing type field" };
232: 		}
233: 		return {
234: 			ok: true,
235: 			event: {
236: 				directory: parsed.directory,
237: 				payload: {
238: 					type: parsed.payload.type,
239: 					properties: parsed.payload.properties ?? {},
240: 				},
241: 			},
242: 		};
243: 	} catch (e) {
244: 		return { ok: false, error: `JSON parse error: ${(e as Error).message}` };
245: 	}
246: }
247: 
248: /**
249:  * Parse a raw SSE data string, auto-detecting the format.
250:  * Handles both the global/wrapped format from OpenCode's /event endpoint
251:  * ({ directory?, payload: { type, properties } }) and the direct format
252:  * ({ type, properties }). Never throws.
253:  */
254: export function parseSSEDataAuto(raw: string): ParseResult {
255: 	if (!raw || raw.trim() === "") {
256: 		return { ok: false, error: "empty data" };
257: 	}
258: 
259: 	try {
260: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
261: 		if (
262: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
263: 			parsed === null ||
264: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
265: 		) {
266: 			return { ok: false, error: "not an object" };
267: 		}
268: 
269: 		// Global format: { payload: { type, properties }, directory? }
270: 		if (
271: 			typeof parsed.payload === "object" &&
272: 			parsed.payload !== null &&
273: 			typeof parsed.payload.type === "string"
274: 		) {
275: 			return {
276: 				ok: true,
277: 				event: {
278: 					type: parsed.payload.type,
279: 					properties: parsed.payload.properties ?? {},
280: 				},
281: 			};
282: 		}
283: 
284: 		// Direct format: { type, properties }
285: 		if (typeof parsed.type === "string") {
286: 			return {
287: 				ok: true,
288: 				event: {
289: 					type: parsed.type,
290: 					properties: parsed.properties ?? {},
291: 				},
292: 			};
293: 		}
294: 
295: 		return { ok: false, error: "unrecognized event format" };
296: 	} catch (e) {
297: 		return { ok: false, error: `JSON parse error: ${(e as Error).message}` };
298: 	}
299: }

301: // ─── Known event types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
302: 
303: const KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES = new Set([
304: 	"message.part.updated",
305: 	"message.part.delta",
306: 	"message.part.removed",
307: 	"message.updated",
308: 	"message.removed",
309: 	"session.status",
310: 	"permission.asked",
311: 	"permission.replied",
312: 	"question.asked",
313: 	"question.replied",
314: 	"question.rejected",
315: 	"pty.created",
316: 	"pty.updated",
317: 	"pty.exited",
318: 	"pty.deleted",
319: 	"file.edited",
320: 	"file.watcher.updated",
321: 	"server.connected",
322: 	"server.heartbeat",
323: ]);
324: 
325: /**
326:  * Check if an event type is known/handled by the relay.
327:  */
328: export function isKnownEventType(type: string): boolean {
329: 	return KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES.has(type);
330: }
331: 
332: /**
333:  * Classify an event type into its category.
334:  */
335: export function classifyEventType(
336: 	type: string,
337: ):
338: 	| "message"
339: 	| "session"
340: 	| "permission"
341: 	| "question"
342: 	| "pty"
343: 	| "file"
344: 	| "server"
345: 	| "unknown" {
346: 	if (type.startsWith("message.")) return "message";
347: 	if (type.startsWith("session.")) return "session";
348: 	if (type.startsWith("permission.")) return "permission";
349: 	if (type.startsWith("question.")) return "question";
350: 	if (type.startsWith("pty.")) return "pty";
351: 	if (type.startsWith("file.")) return "file";
352: 	if (type.startsWith("server.")) return "server";
353: 	return "unknown";
354: }

106: /** Send a JSON message over the WebSocket. */
107: export function wsSend(data: Record<string, unknown>): void {
108: 	if (_ws && _ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
109: 		_ws.send(JSON.stringify(data));
110: 	}
111: }

113: /** Set connection status with tooltip text. */
114: function setStatus(status: ConnectionStatus, text: string): void {

116: 	wsState.statusText = text;

119: // ─── Connection lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
120: 
121: /** Connect callbacks — called after connection established. */
122: let _onConnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
123: let _onDisconnectFn: (() => void) | null = null;
124: 
125: export function onConnect(fn: () => void): void {
126: 	_onConnectFn = fn;
127: }

129: export function onDisconnect(fn: () => void): void {

133: /** Current slug — stored for auto-reconnect so it reconnects to the right project. */
134: let _currentSlug: string | undefined;

136: /** Establish WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect. */
137: export function connect(slug?: string): void {
138: 	_currentSlug = slug;
139: 
140: 	if (_ws) {
141: 		// Remove event listeners to prevent the old socket's close handler
142: 		// from interfering with the new connection (race condition fix).
143: 		const oldWs = _ws;
144: 		_ws = null;
145: 		oldWs.close();
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
149: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
150: 		_reconnectTimer = null;
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	const protocol = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
154: 	const path = slug ? `/p/${slug}/ws` : "/ws";
155: 	const url = `${protocol}//${window.location.host}${path}`;
156: 
157: 	const ws = new WebSocket(url);
158: 	_ws = ws;
159: 
160: 	ws.addEventListener("open", () => {
161: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket
162: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
163: 		setStatus("connected", "Connected");
164: 		_onConnectFn?.();
165: 	});
166: 
167: 	ws.addEventListener("close", () => {
168: 		// Guard: only act if this is still the current socket.
169: 		// If connect() was called again, _ws points to the new socket
170: 		// and we must not null it or start a reconnect timer.
171: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
172: 
173: 		setStatus("disconnected", "Disconnected");
174: 		_ws = null;
175: 
176: 		// Reset chat streaming/processing state so UI isn't stuck
177: 		chatState.streaming = false;
178: 		chatState.processing = false;
179: 
180: 		_onDisconnectFn?.();
181: 
182: 		// Auto-reconnect (use stored slug so we reconnect to the right project)
183: 		if (_reconnectTimer) clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
184: 		_reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
185: 			_reconnectTimer = null;
186: 			connect(_currentSlug);
187: 		}, RECONNECT_DELAY_MS);
188: 	});
189: 
190: 	ws.addEventListener("error", () => {
191: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
192: 		setStatus("error", "Connection error");
193: 	});
194: 
195: 	ws.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
196: 		if (_ws !== ws) return;
197: 		try {
198: 			const msg = JSON.parse(event.data) as RelayMessage;
199: 			handleMessage(msg);
200: 		} catch {
201: 			console.warn("[ws] Failed to parse message:", event.data);
202: 		}
203: 	});
204: }

206: /** Disconnect and stop reconnecting. */
207: export function disconnect(): void {
208: 	if (_reconnectTimer) {
209: 		clearTimeout(_reconnectTimer);
210: 		_reconnectTimer = null;

213: 		_ws.close();

420: // ─── Event Replay (session switch with cached events) ────────────────────────
421: // Replays raw events through existing chat handlers — identical code paths
422: // as live streaming. Zero conversion, full fidelity, handles mid-stream.

424: function replayEvents(events: RelayMessage[]): void {
425: 	chatState.replaying = true;
426: 	for (const event of events) {
427: 		switch (event.type) {
428: 			case "user_message":

55: /** Base error class for all relay errors */
56: export class RelayError extends Error {
57: 	readonly code: ErrorCode;
58: 	readonly statusCode: number;
59: 	readonly userVisible: boolean;
60: 	readonly context: Record<string, unknown>;
61: 
62: 	constructor(
63: 		message: string,
64: 		options: {
65: 			code: ErrorCode;
66: 			statusCode?: number;
67: 			userVisible?: boolean;
68: 			context?: Record<string, unknown>;
69: 			cause?: Error;
70: 		},
71: 	) {
72: 		super(message, { cause: options.cause });
73: 		this.name = "RelayError";
74: 		this.code = options.code;
75: 		this.statusCode = options.statusCode ?? 500;
76: 		this.userVisible = options.userVisible ?? true;
77: 		this.context = options.context ?? {};
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	/** HTTP JSON response shape */
81: 	toJSON(): { error: { code: string; message: string; details?: unknown } } {
82: 		const details =
83: 			Object.keys(this.context).length > 0 ? this.context : undefined;
84: 		return {
85: 			error: {
86: 				code: this.code,
87: 				message: this.message,
88: 				...(details ? { details } : {}),
89: 			},
90: 		};
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	/** WebSocket error message shape (AC1: consistent { type, code, message }) */
94: 	toWebSocket(): { type: "error"; code: string; message: string } {
95: 		return {
96: 			type: "error",
97: 			code: this.code,
98: 			message: this.message,
99: 		};
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	/** Alias for toWebSocket() — returns a RelayMessage error variant (AC1). */
103: 	toMessage(): Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "error" }> {
104: 		return {
105: 			type: "error",
106: 			code: this.code,
107: 			message: this.message,
108: 		};
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	/** Log-safe representation (redacts sensitive data) (AC6) */
112: 	toLog(): Record<string, unknown> {
113: 		return {
114: 			code: this.code,
115: 			message: this.message,
116: 			context: redactSensitive(this.context),
117: 			...(this.cause instanceof Error
118: 				? { cause: { message: this.cause.message, stack: this.cause.stack } }
119: 				: {}),
120: 		};
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	/**
124: 	 * Create a RelayError from any caught value (AC4: error translation).
125: 	 * Replaces the standalone `buildErrorResponse()` utility — same behavior
126: 	 * but produces a proper RelayError instance instead of a plain object.
127: 	 */
128: 	static fromCaught(
129: 		err: unknown,
130: 		code: ErrorCode,
131: 		prefix?: string,
132: 	): RelayError {
133: 		const detail = formatErrorDetail(err);
134: 		const message = prefix ? `${prefix}: ${detail}` : detail;
135: 		const cause = err instanceof Error ? err : undefined;
136: 		return new RelayError(message, { code, cause });
137: 	}
138: }

155: export class OpenCodeApiError extends RelayError {
156: 	readonly endpoint: string;
157: 	readonly responseStatus: number;
158: 	readonly responseBody: unknown;
159: 
160: 	constructor(
161: 		message: string,
162: 		options: {
163: 			endpoint: string;
164: 			responseStatus: number;
165: 			responseBody?: unknown;
166: 			cause?: Error;
167: 		},
168: 	) {
169: 		// For 4xx client errors, include the response body in the message
170: 		// so callers see actionable details (e.g. Zod validation errors)
171: 		let enrichedMessage = message;
172: 		if (
173: 			options.responseBody &&
174: 			options.responseStatus >= 400 &&
175: 			options.responseStatus < 500
176: 		) {
177: 			const bodyStr =
178: 				typeof options.responseBody === "string"
179: 					? options.responseBody
180: 					: JSON.stringify(options.responseBody);
181: 			if (bodyStr.length <= 500) {
182: 				enrichedMessage = `${message}: ${bodyStr}`;
183: 			}
184: 		}
185: 
186: 		super(enrichedMessage, {
187: 			code: "OPENCODE_API_ERROR",
188: 			statusCode: options.responseStatus >= 500 ? 502 : options.responseStatus,
189: 			context: {
190: 				endpoint: options.endpoint,
191: 				responseStatus: options.responseStatus,
192: 				responseBody: options.responseBody,
193: 			},
194: 			cause: options.cause,
195: 		});
196: 		this.name = "OpenCodeApiError";
197: 		this.endpoint = options.endpoint;
198: 		this.responseStatus = options.responseStatus;
199: 		this.responseBody = options.responseBody;
200: 	}
201: }

263: /** Wrap a low-level error in a RelayError subclass, preserving the cause chain */
264: export function wrapError(
265: 	error: unknown,
266: 	ErrorClass: new (
267: 		message: string,
268: 		options?: { cause?: Error; context?: Record<string, unknown> },
269: 	) => RelayError,
270: 	context?: Record<string, unknown>,
271: ): RelayError {
272: 	const cause = error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error));
273: 	return new ErrorClass(cause.message, { cause, context });
274: }

276: /** Redact sensitive values from a context object */
277: export function redactSensitive(
278: 	obj: Record<string, unknown>,
279: ): Record<string, unknown> {
280: 	const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};
281: 	for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {
282: 		if (SENSITIVE_KEYS.has(key.toLowerCase())) {
283: 			result[key] = "[REDACTED]";
284: 		} else if (
285: 			value !== null &&
286: 			typeof value === "object" &&
287: 			!Array.isArray(value)
288: 		) {
289: 			result[key] = redactSensitive(value as Record<string, unknown>);
290: 		} else {
291: 			result[key] = value;
292: 		}
293: 	}
294: 	return result;
295: }

297: /**
298:  * Extract a log-safe error detail string from any caught value.
299:  * For OpenCodeApiError, includes the response body for diagnostics.
300:  */
301: export function formatErrorDetail(err: unknown): string {
302: 	if (err instanceof OpenCodeApiError && err.responseBody) {
303: 		const body =
304: 			typeof err.responseBody === "string"
305: 				? err.responseBody
306: 				: JSON.stringify(err.responseBody);
307: 		return `${err.message} — ${body}`;
308: 	}
309: 	if (err instanceof Error) return err.message;
310: 	if (typeof err === "string") return err;
311: 	return "Unknown error";
312: }

<content>1: // ─── Permissions Store ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Pending permission requests and user questions.
3: 
4: import type {
5: 	AskUserQuestion,
6: 	PermissionRequest,
7: 	QuestionRequest,
8: 	RelayMessage,
9: } from "../types.js";
10: 
11: // ─── State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12: 
13: export const permissionsState = $state({
14: 	pendingPermissions: [] as (PermissionRequest & { id: string })[],
15: 	pendingQuestions: [] as QuestionRequest[],
16: 	/** Tools that user has "always allowed" in this session. */
17: 	alwaysAllowedTools: new Set<string>(),
18: });
19: 
20: // ─── Derived getters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: // Components should wrap in $derived() for reactive caching.
22: 
23: /** Get the total number of pending items requiring user attention. */
24: export function getPendingCount(): number {
25: 	return (
26: 		permissionsState.pendingPermissions.length +
27: 		permissionsState.pendingQuestions.length
28: 	);
29: }
30: 
31: /** Get whether there are any pending permissions or questions. */
32: export function getHasPending(): boolean {
33: 	return getPendingCount() > 0;
34: }
35: 
36: // ─── Pure helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37: 
38: /** Build the answer payload for a question response. */
39: export function buildAnswerPayload(
40: 	selections: Map<number, string>,
41: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
42: ): Record<string, string> {
43: 	const answers: Record<string, string> = {};
44: 	for (const [idx, value] of selections) {
45: 		if (idx < questions.length) {
46: 			answers[questions[idx]!.question] = value;
47: 		}
48: 	}
49: 	return answers;
50: }
51: 
52: /** Check if all questions can be auto-submitted (single option each). */
53: export function shouldAutoSubmit(questions: AskUserQuestion[]): boolean {
54: 	return questions.every(
55: 		(q) => q.options.length === 1 && !q.multiSelect && !q.custom,
56: 	);
57: }
58: 
59: /** Check if all required questions have been answered. */
60: export function isValidSubmission(
61: 	selections: Map<number, string>,
62: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
63: ): boolean {
64: 	for (let i = 0; i < questions.length; i++) {
65: 		if (!selections.has(i)) return false;
66: 	}
67: 	return true;
68: }
69: 
70: /** Format a question header for display. */
71: export function formatQuestionHeader(header: string): string {
72: 	// Capitalize first letter
73: 	if (!header) return "";
74: 	return header.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + header.slice(1);
75: }
76: 
77: // ─── Message handlers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78: 
79: export function handlePermissionRequest(
80: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "permission_request" }>,
81: 	sendFn?: (data: Record<string, unknown>) => void,
82: ): void {
83: 	const { requestId, toolName, toolInput } = msg;
84: 
85: 	if (!requestId || !toolName) return;
86: 
87: 	// Check if already always-allowed — auto-approve and send response
88: 	if (permissionsState.alwaysAllowedTools.has(toolName)) {
89: 		sendFn?.({
90: 			type: "permission_response",
91: 			requestId,
92: 			decision: "allow",
93: 		});
94: 		return;
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	const permission: PermissionRequest & { id: string } = {
98: 		id: requestId,
99: 		requestId,
100: 		toolName,
101: 		toolInput,
102: 	};
103: 
104: 	permissionsState.pendingPermissions = [
105: 		...permissionsState.pendingPermissions,
106: 		permission,
107: 	];
108: }
109: 
110: export function handlePermissionResolved(
111: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "permission_resolved" }>,
112: ): void {
113: 	const { requestId } = msg;
114: 	if (!requestId) return;
115: 
116: 	permissionsState.pendingPermissions =
117: 		permissionsState.pendingPermissions.filter(
118: 			(p) => p.requestId !== requestId,
119: 		);
120: }
121: 
122: export function handleAskUser(
123: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "ask_user" }>,
124: ): void {
125: 	const { toolId, questions } = msg;
126: 
127: 	if (!toolId || !Array.isArray(questions)) return;
128: 
129: 	const request: QuestionRequest = { toolId, questions };
130: 	permissionsState.pendingQuestions = [
131: 		...permissionsState.pendingQuestions,
132: 		request,
133: 	];
134: }
135: 
136: export function handleAskUserResolved(
137: 	msg: Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "ask_user_resolved" }>,
138: ): void {
139: 	const { toolId } = msg;
140: 	if (!toolId) return;
141: 
142: 	permissionsState.pendingQuestions = permissionsState.pendingQuestions.filter(
143: 		(q) => q.toolId !== toolId,
144: 	);
145: }
146: 
147: // ─── Actions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
148: 
149: /** Remove a permission request (after responding). */
150: export function removePermission(requestId: string): void {
151: 	permissionsState.pendingPermissions =
152: 		permissionsState.pendingPermissions.filter(
153: 			(p) => p.requestId !== requestId,
154: 		);
155: }
156: 
157: /** Remove a question request (after responding). */
158: export function removeQuestion(toolId: string): void {
159: 	permissionsState.pendingQuestions = permissionsState.pendingQuestions.filter(
160: 		(q) => q.toolId !== toolId,
161: 	);
162: }
163: 
164: /** Mark a tool as always-allowed for this session. */
165: export function alwaysAllowTool(toolName: string): void {
166: 	const next = new Set(permissionsState.alwaysAllowedTools);
167: 	next.add(toolName);
168: 	permissionsState.alwaysAllowedTools = next;
169: }
170: 
171: /** Clear all pending items (e.g. on disconnect). */
172: export function clearAll(): void {
173: 	permissionsState.pendingPermissions = [];
174: 	permissionsState.pendingQuestions = [];
175: }
176: 
177: /** Clear all permissions state including always-allowed tools (for project switch). */
178: export function clearAllPermissions(): void {
179: 	permissionsState.pendingPermissions = [];
180: 	permissionsState.pendingQuestions = [];
181: 	permissionsState.alwaysAllowedTools = new Set<string>();
182: }

<content>1: // ─── Question / Ask-User Bridge (Ticket 1.6) ────────────────────────────────
2: 
3: import type { AskUserQuestion, OpenCodeEvent } from "./types.js";
4: 
5: export interface PendingQuestion {
6: 	toolId: string;
7: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[];
8: 	timestamp: number;
9: }
10: 
11: export interface QuestionBridgeOptions {
12: 	timeoutMs?: number;
13: 	now?: () => number;
14: }
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Convert browser answer format to OpenCode's format.
18:  * Browser: { "0": "Option A", "1": "Option B" }
19:  * OpenCode: [["Option A"], ["Option B"]]
20:  *
21:  * For multi-select:
22:  * Browser: { "0": "Opt A, Opt B" }
23:  * OpenCode: [["Opt A", "Opt B"]]
24:  */
25: export function convertAnswers(
26: 	answers: Record<string, string>,
27: 	questions: AskUserQuestion[],
28: ): string[][] {
29: 	const result: string[][] = [];
30: 
31: 	for (let i = 0; i < questions.length; i++) {
32: 		const key = String(i);
33: 		const raw = answers[key];
34: 
35: 		if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
36: 			result.push([]);
37: 			continue;
38: 		}
39: 
40: 		if (questions[i]!.multiSelect) {
41: 			// Split comma-separated selections
42: 			result.push(
43: 				raw
44: 					.split(",")
45: 					.map((s) => s.trim())
46: 					.filter((s) => s.length > 0),
47: 			);
48: 		} else {
49: 			result.push([raw]);
50: 		}
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return result;
54: }
55: 
56: /**
57:  * Map OpenCode question format (with `multiple` field) to frontend format (with `multiSelect` field).
58:  */
59: export function mapQuestionFields(
60: 	ocQuestions: Array<{
61: 		question?: string;
62: 		header?: string;
63: 		options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
64: 		multiple?: boolean;
65: 		custom?: boolean;
66: 	}>,
67: ): AskUserQuestion[] {
68: 	return ocQuestions.map((q) => ({
69: 		question: q.question ?? "",
70: 		header: q.header ?? "",
71: 		options: (q.options ?? []).map((o) => ({
72: 			label: o.label ?? "",
73: 			description: o.description ?? "",
74: 		})),
75: 		multiSelect: q.multiple ?? false,
76: 		custom: q.custom ?? true,
77: 	}));
78: }
79: 
80: export class QuestionBridge {
81: 	private pending: Map<string, PendingQuestion> = new Map();
82: 	private readonly timeoutMs: number;
83: 	private readonly now: () => number;
84: 
85: 	constructor(options: QuestionBridgeOptions = {}) {
86: 		this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs ?? 10 * 60_000; // 10 minutes
87: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	/** Process an incoming question.asked SSE event */
91: 	onQuestion(event: OpenCodeEvent): PendingQuestion | null {
92: 		const props = event.properties as {
93: 			id?: string;
94: 			questions?: Array<{
95: 				question?: string;
96: 				header?: string;
97: 				options?: Array<{ label?: string; description?: string }>;
98: 				multiple?: boolean;
99: 				custom?: boolean;
100: 			}>;
101: 		};
102: 
103: 		if (!props.id || !props.questions) return null;
104: 
105: 		const questions = mapQuestionFields(props.questions);
106: 		const entry: PendingQuestion = {
107: 			toolId: props.id,
108: 			questions,
109: 			timestamp: this.now(),
110: 		};
111: 
112: 		this.pending.set(props.id, entry);
113: 		return entry;
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	/** Process a user's answer from the browser */
117: 	onAnswer(
118: 		toolId: string,
119: 		answers: Record<string, string>,
120: 	): { formatted: string[][] } | null {
121: 		const entry = this.pending.get(toolId);
122: 		if (!entry) return null;
123: 
124: 		const formatted = convertAnswers(answers, entry.questions);
125: 		this.pending.delete(toolId);
126: 		return { formatted };
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	/** Get all pending questions (for replay on reconnect) */
130: 	getPending(): PendingQuestion[] {
131: 		return Array.from(this.pending.values());
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	/** Check for timed-out questions */
135: 	checkTimeouts(): string[] {
136: 		const now = this.now();
137: 		const timedOut: string[] = [];
138: 
139: 		for (const [id, entry] of this.pending) {
140: 			if (now - entry.timestamp >= this.timeoutMs) {
141: 				timedOut.push(id);
142: 				// NOTE: per spec, questions do NOT auto-answer on timeout
143: 				// but we track them for notification purposes
144: 			}
145: 		}
146: 
147: 		return timedOut;
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	/** Remove a question (e.g., after timeout notification) */
151: 	remove(toolId: string): boolean {
152: 		return this.pending.delete(toolId);
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	get size(): number {
156: 		return this.pending.size;
157: 	}
158: }

2: // Main HTTP server: serves frontend assets, handles PIN auth, routes projects,
3: // upgrades WebSocket connections. Delegates all HTTP routing to RequestRouter.

5: import {

14: import { AuthManager } from "./auth.js";

16: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
17: 
18: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: 
20: export interface RelayServerOptions {
21: 	port?: number;
22: 	host?: string;
23: 	staticDir?: string;
24: 	pin?: string;

35: 	onUpgrade?: (req: IncomingMessage, socket: unknown, head: Buffer) => void;
36: 	/** Called for API requests under this project */
37: 	onApiRequest?: (
38: 		req: IncomingMessage,

40: 		path: string,

49: // ─── Server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50: 
51: export class RelayServer {
52: 	private server: Server | null = null;
53: 	private readonly port: number;
54: 	private readonly host: string;
55: 	private readonly auth: AuthManager;
56: 	private readonly projects: Map<string, ProjectEntry> = new Map();
57: 	private readonly tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
58: 	private readonly protocol: "https" | "http";
59: 	private readonly router: RequestRouter;
60: 
61: 	constructor(options: RelayServerOptions = {}) {
62: 		this.port = options.port ?? 2633;
63: 		this.host = options.host ?? "0.0.0.0";
64: 		this.auth = new AuthManager();
65: 		this.tls = options.tls;
66: 		this.protocol = this.tls ? "https" : "http";
67: 
68: 		if (options.pin) {
69: 			this.auth.setPin(options.pin);
70: 		}
71: 
72: 		const staticDir =
73: 			options.staticDir ?? join(process.cwd(), "dist", "public");
74: 
75: 		this.router = new RequestRouter({
76: 			auth: this.auth,
77: 			staticDir,
78: 			getProjects: () =>
79: 				Array.from(this.projects.values()).map((p) => ({
80: 					slug: p.slug,
81: 					directory: p.directory,
82: 					title: p.title,
83: 				})),
84: 			port: this.port,
85: 			isTls: this.protocol === "https",
86: 			pushManager: options.pushManager,
87: 			caRootPath: this.tls?.caRoot,
88: 			onProjectApiRequest: (slug, req, res, subPath) => {
89: 				const project = this.projects.get(slug);
90: 				if (project?.onApiRequest) {
91: 					project.onApiRequest(req, res, subPath);
92: 					return true;
93: 				}
94: 				return false;
95: 			},
96: 		});
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	/** Register a project for slug-based routing */
100: 	addProject(project: ProjectEntry): void {
101: 		this.projects.set(project.slug, project);
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/** Remove a project */
105: 	removeProject(slug: string): boolean {
106: 		return this.projects.delete(slug);
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	/** Get all registered projects */
110: 	getProjects(): ProjectEntry[] {
111: 		return Array.from(this.projects.values());
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	/** Get the auth manager (for external PIN management) */
115: 	getAuth(): AuthManager {
116: 		return this.auth;
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Get the request router (for external checkAuth access) */
120: 	getRouter(): RequestRouter {
121: 		return this.router;
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Returns true when the server is configured to use TLS */
125: 	isTls(): boolean {
126: 		return this.protocol === "https";
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	/** Start the server */
130: 	async start(): Promise<void> {
131: 		return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
132: 			const handler = (req: IncomingMessage, res: ServerResponse) =>
133: 				this.router.handleRequest(req, res);
134: 
135: 			this.server = this.tls
136: 				? createHttpsServer({ key: this.tls.key, cert: this.tls.cert }, handler)
137: 				: createServer(handler);
138: 
139: 			this.server.on("error", (err) => {
140: 				reject(err);
141: 			});
142: 
143: 			this.server.listen(this.port, this.host, () => {
144: 				resolve();
145: 			});
146: 		});
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	/** Stop the server */
150: 	async stop(): Promise<void> {
151: 		return new Promise((resolve) => {
152: 			if (!this.server) {
153: 				resolve();
154: 				return;
155: 			}
156: 			this.server.close(() => {
157: 				this.server = null;
158: 				resolve();
159: 			});
160: 		});
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	/** Get the underlying HTTP server (for WebSocket upgrade) */
164: 	getHttpServer(): Server | null {
165: 		return this.server;
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	/** Get server URLs */
169: 	getUrls(): ServerUrls {
170: 		const local = `${this.protocol}://localhost:${this.port}`;
171: 		const network: string[] = [];
172: 
173: 		const ifaces = networkInterfaces();
174: 		for (const entries of Object.values(ifaces)) {
175: 			if (!entries) continue;
176: 			for (const entry of entries) {
177: 				if (entry.family === "IPv4" && !entry.internal) {
178: 					network.push(`${this.protocol}://${entry.address}:${this.port}`);
179: 				}
180: 			}
181: 		}
182: 
183: 		return { local, network };
184: 	}
185: }

67: // ─── RequestRouter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68: 
69: export class RequestRouter {
70: 	private readonly auth: AuthManager;
71: 	private readonly staticDir: string;
72: 	private readonly getProjects: () => RouterProject[];
73: 	private readonly port: number;
74: 	private readonly isTls: boolean;
75: 	private readonly pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
76: 	private readonly authExemptPaths: string[];
77: 	private readonly getHealthResponse?: () => object;
78: 	private readonly onProjectApiRequest?: RequestRouterDeps["onProjectApiRequest"];
79: 	private readonly caRootPath?: string;
80: 
81: 	constructor(deps: RequestRouterDeps) {
82: 		this.auth = deps.auth;
83: 		this.staticDir = deps.staticDir;
84: 		this.getProjects = deps.getProjects;
85: 		this.port = deps.port;
86: 		this.isTls = deps.isTls;
87: 		this.pushManager = deps.pushManager;
88: 		this.authExemptPaths = deps.authExemptPaths ?? [];
89: 		this.getHealthResponse = deps.getHealthResponse;
90: 		this.onProjectApiRequest = deps.onProjectApiRequest;
91: 		this.caRootPath = deps.caRootPath;
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	// ─── Main entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────
95: 
96: 	async handleRequest(
97: 		req: IncomingMessage,
98: 		res: ServerResponse,
99: 	): Promise<void> {
100: 		const url = new URL(
101: 			req.url ?? "/",
102: 			`http://${req.headers.host ?? "localhost"}`,
103: 		);
104: 		const pathname = url.pathname;
105: 
106: 		try {
107: 			// CORS headers on every response
108: 			res.setHeader("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
109: 			res.setHeader(
110: 				"Access-Control-Allow-Methods",
111: 				"GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS",
112: 			);
113: 			res.setHeader(
114: 				"Access-Control-Allow-Headers",
115: 				"Content-Type, Authorization, X-Relay-Pin",
116: 			);
117: 
118: 			if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
119: 				res.writeHead(204);
120: 				res.end();
121: 				return;
122: 			}
123: 
124: 			// ─── Auth endpoint (always accessible) ──────────────────────
125: 			if (pathname === "/auth" && req.method === "POST") {
126: 				await this.handleAuth(req, res);
127: 				return;
128: 			}
129: 
130: 			// ─── Auth status API (before auth gate — tells client if auth is needed) ──
131: 			if (pathname === "/api/auth/status" && req.method === "GET") {
132: 				const hasPin = this.auth.hasPin();
133: 				const authenticated = hasPin ? this.checkAuth(req) : true;
134: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
135: 				res.end(JSON.stringify({ hasPin, authenticated }));
136: 				return;
137: 			}
138: 
139: 			// ─── Auth gate (if PIN is set) ──────────────────────────────
140: 			if (this.auth.hasPin()) {
141: 				const authed = this.checkAuth(req);
142: 				if (!authed) {
143: 					const isExempt = this.authExemptPaths.includes(pathname);
144: 					if (!isExempt) {
145: 						const isBrowserRoute =
146: 							pathname === "/" ||
147: 							pathname === "" ||
148: 							pathname === "/auth" ||
149: 							pathname.startsWith("/p/");
150: 						const isApiRoute = pathname.startsWith("/api/");
151: 						const hasPinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"] !== undefined;
152: 
153: 						if (isApiRoute || hasPinHeader) {
154: 							res.writeHead(401, {
155: 								"Content-Type": "application/json",
156: 							});
157: 							res.end(
158: 								JSON.stringify({
159: 									error: {
160: 										code: "AUTH_REQUIRED",
161: 										message: "PIN required",
162: 									},
163: 								}),
164: 							);
165: 							return;
166: 						}
167: 						if (isBrowserRoute) {
168: 							if (pathname !== "/auth") {
169: 								res.writeHead(302, { Location: "/auth" });
170: 								res.end();
171: 								return;
172: 							}
173: 							// /auth itself serves the Svelte SPA
174: 							await this.serveStaticFile(res, "index.html");
175: 							return;
176: 						}
177: 						// Static files fall through so the login page can load assets
178: 					}
179: 				}
180: 			}
181: 
182: 			// ─── Auth page (GET /auth — serves Svelte PinPage) ──────────
183: 			if (pathname === "/auth" && req.method === "GET") {
184: 				await this.serveStaticFile(res, "index.html");
185: 				return;
186: 			}
187: 
188: 			// ─── Setup page (serves Svelte SetupPage) ───────────────────
189: 			if (pathname === "/setup") {
190: 				await this.serveStaticFile(res, "index.html");
191: 				return;
192: 			}
193: 
194: 			// ─── Setup info API ─────────────────────────────────────────
195: 			if (pathname === "/api/setup-info" && req.method === "GET") {
196: 				const lanMode = url.searchParams.get("mode") === "lan";
197: 				const host = req.headers.host ?? `localhost:${this.port}`;
198: 				const hostBase = host.replace(/:\d+$/, "");
199: 				const httpsUrl = `https://${hostBase}:${this.port}`;
200: 				const httpUrl = `http://${hostBase}:${this.port}`;
201: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
202: 				res.end(
203: 					JSON.stringify({
204: 						httpsUrl,
205: 						httpUrl,
206: 						hasCert: this.isTls,
207: 						lanMode,
208: 					}),
209: 				);
210: 				return;
211: 			}
212: 
213: 			// ─── Health check (/health and /api/status) ─────────────────
214: 			if (pathname === "/health" || pathname === "/api/status") {
215: 				const body = this.getHealthResponse
216: 					? this.getHealthResponse()
217: 					: {
218: 							ok: true,
219: 							projects: this.getProjects().length,
220: 							uptime: process.uptime(),
221: 						};
222: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
223: 				res.end(JSON.stringify(body));
224: 				return;
225: 			}
226: 
227: 			// ─── Info endpoint ───────────────────────────────────────────
228: 			if (pathname === "/info") {
229: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
230: 				res.end(JSON.stringify({ version: getVersion() }));
231: 				return;
232: 			}
233: 
234: 			// ─── Projects list API ──────────────────────────────────────
235: 			if (pathname === "/api/projects" && req.method === "GET") {
236: 				const projects = this.getProjects();
237: 				const dashProjects = projects.map((p) => ({
238: 					slug: p.slug,
239: 					path: p.directory,
240: 					title: p.title || undefined,
241: 					sessions: 0,
242: 					clients: 0,
243: 					isProcessing: false,
244: 				}));
245: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
246: 				res.end(
247: 					JSON.stringify({
248: 						projects: dashProjects,
249: 						version: getVersion(),
250: 					}),
251: 				);
252: 				return;
253: 			}
254: 
255: 			// ─── Push notification API ──────────────────────────────────
256: 			if (await this.handlePushRoutes(pathname, req, res)) {
257: 				return;
258: 			}
259: 
260: 			// ─── CA certificate download ────────────────────────────────
261: 			if (pathname === "/ca/download") {
262: 				await this.handleCaDownload(res);
263: 				return;
264: 			}
265: 
266: 			// ─── Project routes: /p/{slug}/... ──────────────────────────
267: 			const projectMatch = pathname.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)(\/.*)?$/);
268: 			if (projectMatch) {
269: 				const slug = projectMatch[1];
270: 				const subPath = projectMatch[2] ?? "/";
271: 				const projects = this.getProjects();
272: 				const project = projects.find((p) => p.slug === slug);
273: 
274: 				if (!project) {
275: 					res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
276: 					res.end(
277: 						JSON.stringify({
278: 							error: {
279: 								code: "NOT_FOUND",
280: 								message: `Project "${slug}" not found`,
281: 							},
282: 						}),
283: 					);
284: 					return;
285: 				}
286: 
287: 				// API sub-routes — delegate if handler provided
288: 				if (subPath.startsWith("/api/") && this.onProjectApiRequest) {
289: 					const handled = this.onProjectApiRequest(
290: 						slug!,
291: 						req,
292: 						res,
293: 						subPath.slice(4),
294: 					);
295: 					if (handled) return;
296: 				}
297: 
298: 				// Serve index.html for SPA routes
299: 				await this.serveStaticFile(res, "index.html");
300: 				return;
301: 			}
302: 
303: 			// ─── Static files ───────────────────────────────────────────
304: 			if (pathname !== "/" && pathname !== "") {
305: 				const served = await this.tryServeStatic(res, pathname.slice(1));
306: 				if (served) return;
307: 			}
308: 
309: 			// ─── Root: single-project redirect or dashboard ─────────────
310: 			const projects = this.getProjects();
311: 			if (projects.length === 1) {
312: 				const slug = projects[0]!.slug;
313: 				res.writeHead(302, { Location: `/p/${slug}/` });
314: 				res.end();
315: 				return;
316: 			}
317: 
318: 			// 0 or multiple projects — serve Svelte dashboard
319: 			await this.serveStaticFile(res, "index.html");
320: 		} catch (err) {
321: 			console.error("[router] Request error:", err);
322: 			if (!res.headersSent) {
323: 				res.writeHead(500, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
324: 				res.end(
325: 					JSON.stringify({
326: 						error: {
327: 							code: "INTERNAL_ERROR",
328: 							message: "Internal server error",
329: 						},
330: 					}),
331: 				);
332: 			}
333: 		}
334: 	}
335: 
336: 	// ─── Auth (public — needed by WS upgrade handlers) ──────────────────
337: 
338: 	checkAuth(req: IncomingMessage): boolean {
339: 		// Check cookie
340: 		const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
341: 		const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
342: 		if (sessionCookie && this.auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)) {
343: 			return true;
344: 		}
345: 
346: 		// Check X-Relay-Pin header
347: 		const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
348: 		if (typeof pinHeader === "string") {
349: 			const ip = getClientIp(req);
350: 			const result = this.auth.authenticate(pinHeader, ip);
351: 			return result.ok;
352: 		}
353: 
354: 		return false;
355: 	}
356: 
357: 	// ─── Private: Auth handler ──────────────────────────────────────────
358: 
359: 	private async handleAuth(
360: 		req: IncomingMessage,
361: 		res: ServerResponse,
362: 	): Promise<void> {
363: 		const body = await readBody(req);
364: 		let pin: string;
365: 
366: 		try {
367: 			const data = JSON.parse(body);
368: 			pin = String(data.pin ?? "");
369: 		} catch {
370: 			res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
371: 			res.end(
372: 				JSON.stringify({
373: 					error: { code: "BAD_REQUEST", message: "Invalid JSON" },
374: 				}),
375: 			);
376: 			return;
377: 		}
378: 
379: 		const ip = getClientIp(req);
380: 		const result = this.auth.authenticate(pin, ip);
381: 
382: 		if (result.ok) {
383: 			res.writeHead(200, {
384: 				"Content-Type": "application/json",
385: 				"Set-Cookie": `relay_session=${result.cookie}; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age=86400`,
386: 			});
387: 			res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));
388: 		} else if (result.locked) {
389: 			res.writeHead(429, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
390: 			res.end(
391: 				JSON.stringify({
392: 					ok: false,
393: 					locked: true,
394: 					retryAfter: result.retryAfter,
395: 				}),
396: 			);
397: 		} else {
398: 			const attemptsLeft = this.auth.getRemainingAttempts(ip);
399: 			res.writeHead(401, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
400: 			res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: false, attemptsLeft }));
401: 		}
402: 	}
403: 
404: 	// ─── Private: Push notification routes ──────────────────────────────
405: 	// Returns true if the route was handled (caller should return), false otherwise.
406: 
407: 	private async handlePushRoutes(
408: 		pathname: string,
409: 		req: IncomingMessage,
410: 		res: ServerResponse,
411: 	): Promise<boolean> {
412: 		if (pathname === "/api/push/vapid-key" && req.method === "GET") {
413: 			const publicKey = this.pushManager?.getPublicKey();
414: 			if (!publicKey) {
415: 				res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
416: 				res.end(
417: 					JSON.stringify({
418: 						error: {
419: 							code: "NOT_AVAILABLE",
420: 							message: "Push notifications not available",
421: 						},
422: 					}),
423: 				);
424: 			} else {
425: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
426: 				res.end(JSON.stringify({ publicKey }));
427: 			}
428: 			return true;
429: 		}
430: 
431: 		if (pathname === "/api/push/subscribe" && req.method === "POST") {
432: 			if (!this.pushManager) {
433: 				res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
434: 				res.end(
435: 					JSON.stringify({
436: 						error: {
437: 							code: "NOT_AVAILABLE",
438: 							message: "Push notifications not available",
439: 						},
440: 					}),
441: 				);
442: 				return true;
443: 			}
444: 			const body = await readBody(req);
445: 			try {
446: 				const { subscription } = JSON.parse(body);
447: 				if (!subscription?.endpoint) {
448: 					res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
449: 					res.end(
450: 						JSON.stringify({
451: 							error: {
452: 								code: "BAD_REQUEST",
453: 								message: "Missing subscription endpoint",
454: 							},
455: 						}),
456: 					);
457: 					return true;
458: 				}
459: 				this.pushManager.addSubscription(subscription.endpoint, subscription);
460: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
461: 				res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));
462: 			} catch {
463: 				res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
464: 				res.end(
465: 					JSON.stringify({
466: 						error: { code: "BAD_REQUEST", message: "Invalid JSON" },
467: 					}),
468: 				);
469: 			}
470: 			return true;
471: 		}
472: 
473: 		if (pathname === "/api/push/unsubscribe" && req.method === "POST") {
474: 			if (!this.pushManager) {
475: 				res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
476: 				res.end(
477: 					JSON.stringify({
478: 						error: {
479: 							code: "NOT_AVAILABLE",
480: 							message: "Push notifications not available",
481: 						},
482: 					}),
483: 				);
484: 				return true;
485: 			}
486: 			const body = await readBody(req);
487: 			try {
488: 				const { endpoint } = JSON.parse(body);
489: 				if (!endpoint) {
490: 					res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
491: 					res.end(
492: 						JSON.stringify({
493: 							error: {
494: 								code: "BAD_REQUEST",
495: 								message: "Missing endpoint",
496: 							},
497: 						}),
498: 					);
499: 					return true;
500: 				}
501: 				this.pushManager.removeSubscription(endpoint);
502: 				res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });

521: 		if (!this.caRootPath) {
522: 			res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
523: 			res.end(
524: 				JSON.stringify({
525: 					error: {
526: 						code: "NOT_FOUND",
527: 						message: "No CA certificate available",
528: 					},
529: 				}),
530: 			);
531: 			return;
532: 		}
533: 		try {
534: 			const pem = await readFile(this.caRootPath);
535: 			res.writeHead(200, {
536: 				"Content-Type": "application/x-pem-file",
537: 				"Content-Disposition": 'attachment; filename="opencode-relay-ca.pem"',
538: 				"Content-Length": pem.length,
539: 			});
540: 			res.end(pem);
541: 		} catch {
542: 			res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
543: 			res.end(
544: 				JSON.stringify({

556: 	/** Cache-control header value based on whether the file path contains a content hash. */
557: 	private getCacheControl(filePath: string): string {
558: 		return filePath.includes(".") && /\.[a-f0-9]{8,}\./.test(filePath)
559: 			? "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"
560: 			: "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate";
561: 	}

563: 	private async serveStaticFile(
564: 		res: ServerResponse,
565: 		filePath: string,
566: 	): Promise<void> {
567: 		if (!filePath || filePath === "") filePath = "index.html";
568: 
569: 		// Prevent directory traversal
570: 		const resolved = resolve(this.staticDir, filePath);
571: 		if (!resolved.startsWith(resolve(this.staticDir))) {
572: 			res.writeHead(403, { "Content-Type": "text/plain" });
573: 			res.end("Forbidden");
574: 			return;
575: 		}
576: 
577: 		try {
578: 			const fileStat = await stat(resolved);
579: 			if (fileStat.isDirectory()) {
580: 				return this.serveStaticFile(res, join(filePath, "index.html"));
581: 			}
582: 
583: 			const content = await readFile(resolved);
584: 			const ext = extname(resolved).toLowerCase();
585: 			const contentType = MIME_TYPES[ext] ?? "application/octet-stream";
586: 
587: 			res.writeHead(200, {
588: 				"Content-Type": contentType,
589: 				"Content-Length": content.length,
590: 				"Cache-Control": this.getCacheControl(filePath),
591: 			});
592: 			res.end(content);
593: 		} catch (err) {
594: 			// SPA fallback: try index.html if original file not found
595: 			if ((err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT") {
596: 				if (filePath !== "index.html") {
597: 					try {
598: 						const indexPath = resolve(this.staticDir, "index.html");
599: 						const content = await readFile(indexPath);
600: 						res.writeHead(200, {
601: 							"Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8",
602: 							"Cache-Control": "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
603: 						});
604: 						res.end(content);
605: 						return;
606: 					} catch {
607: 						// index.html also doesn't exist
608: 					}
609: 				}
610: 				res.writeHead(404, { "Content-Type": "text/plain" });
611: 				res.end("Not Found");
612: 			} else {
613: 				throw err;
614: 			}
615: 		}
616: 	}

618: 	/** Try to serve a static file. Returns true if served, false otherwise. */
619: 	private async tryServeStatic(
620: 		res: ServerResponse,
621: 		filePath: string,
622: 	): Promise<boolean> {
623: 		const resolved = resolve(this.staticDir, filePath);
624: 		if (!resolved.startsWith(resolve(this.staticDir))) return false;
625: 		try {
626: 			const s = await stat(resolved);
627: 			if (!s.isFile()) return false;
628: 			const content = await readFile(resolved);
629: 			const ext = extname(resolved).toLowerCase();
630: 			const contentType = MIME_TYPES[ext] ?? "application/octet-stream";
631: 			res.writeHead(200, {
632: 				"Content-Type": contentType,
633: 				"Content-Length": content.length,
634: 				"Cache-Control": this.getCacheControl(filePath),
635: 			});
636: 			res.end(content);
637: 			return true;
638: 		} catch {

9: Comprehensive error handling for all failure modes. The relay should never crash from expected errors, should provide clear feedback to users, and should recover gracefully when possible.
10: 
11: ## Acceptance Criteria
12: 
13: ### AC1: OpenCode server not running
14: 
15: ```gherkin
16: Given the OpenCode server is not running
17: When the relay starts
18: Then it attempts to start OpenCode via the process manager
19: And if it fails, it shows a clear error page to connected browsers:
20:   "OpenCode server is not running. Start it with: opencode serve"
21: And the relay stays alive (doesn't crash)
22: And it retries connection periodically
23: ```
24: 
25: ### AC2: OpenCode server crashes mid-session
26: 
27: ```gherkin
28: Given the OpenCode server is running and processing a request
29: When the OpenCode process crashes unexpectedly
30: Then the relay detects the SSE connection drop
31: And it sends { type: "error", message: "OpenCode server disconnected" } to browsers
32: And it attempts to restart the OpenCode process
33: And on recovery, browsers are notified: "Reconnected to OpenCode"
34: ```
35: 
36: ### AC3: Browser disconnects and reconnects
37: 
38: ```gherkin
39: Given a browser was connected and receiving events
40: When the browser loses connection (network drop, sleep, etc.)
41: And it reconnects within 5 minutes
42: Then it receives any pending permission requests
43: And it receives the current session state
44: And the chat shows events that were missed during disconnection
45: ```
46: 
47: ### AC4: Multiple browsers sending conflicting permissions
48: 
49: ```gherkin
50: Given a permission request is pending
51: When browser A approves and browser B rejects simultaneously
52: Then only the first response is processed
53: And the second response is ignored (duplicate handling)
54: And both browsers see the same final decision
55: ```
56: 
57: ### AC5: Large tool results
58: 
59: ```gherkin
60: Given a tool result contains more than 100KB of content
61: When it is sent to browsers
62: Then it is truncated to a reasonable size (e.g., first 50KB)
63: And a "Show full output" toggle is available
64: And the full content can be loaded on demand
65: ```
66: 
67: ### AC6: Invalid WebSocket messages
68: 
69: ```gherkin
70: Given a browser sends malformed JSON
71: When the server receives it
72: Then it logs the error
73: And it sends { type: "error", message: "Invalid message format" } to that client
74: And it does NOT disconnect the client
75: And other clients are not affected
76: ```
77: 
78: ### AC7: Rate limiting on message sends
79: 
80: ```gherkin
81: Given a user sends messages rapidly
82: When more than 5 messages are sent within 10 seconds
83: Then subsequent messages are queued (not dropped)
84: And the user sees "Message queued..." feedback
85: And messages are processed in order
86: ```
87: 
88: ### AC8: Disk space for session storage
89: 
90: ```gherkin
91: Given session storage is using disk
92: When disk space is low (< 100MB free)
93: Then the relay logs a warning
94: And it continues operating but warns about potential data loss
95: And old session cache files are candidates for cleanup
96: ```
97: 
98: ## Testable Components
99: 
100: | Component | Test Type | What to Assert |
101: |-----------|-----------|----------------|
102: | OpenCode down | Fixture | Error page shown, retry in background |
103: | OpenCode crash recovery | Fixture | Detects, notifies, restarts, reconnects |
104: | Browser reconnection | Fixture | State replayed, pending items sent |
105: | Duplicate permission | Unit | First processed, second ignored |
106: | Large content truncation | Unit | Truncated at limit, expand available |
107: | Invalid JSON handling | Unit | Error sent, no disconnect |
108: | Message rate limit | Unit | Queued, processed in order |
109: | Disk space check | Unit | Warning logged at threshold |
110: 
111: ## Notes
112: 
113: - Every error should be logged with enough context to debug
114: - User-facing errors should be human-readable (no stack traces)
115: - The relay should NEVER crash from an error in a single client connection
116: - Use process.on('uncaughtException') as a last resort safety net

58:
